<h3 data-section-id="31g7e9" data-start="5118" data-end="5132">Efficiency</h3>
<span class="katex-display"><span class="katex"><span class="katex-mathml"><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="block"><semantics><mrow><mi>η</mi><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mtext>Output</mtext><mtext>Input</mtext></mfrac><mo>×</mo><mn>100</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">%</mi></mrow><annotation encoding="application/x-tex">\eta
=
\frac{\text{Output}}
{\text{Input}}
\times100\%</annotation></semantics></math></span><span class="katex-html" aria-hidden="true"><span class="base"><span class="strut"></span><span class="mord mathnormal">η</span><span class="mspace"></span><span class="mrel">=</span><span class="mspace"></span></span><span class="base"><span class="strut"></span><span class="mord"><span class="mopen nulldelimiter"></span><span class="mfrac"><span class="vlist-t vlist-t2"><span class="vlist-r"><span class="vlist"><span><span class="pstrut"></span><span class="mord"><span class="mord text"><span class="mord">Input</span></span></span></span><span><span class="pstrut"></span><span class="frac-line"></span></span><span><span class="pstrut"></span><span class="mord"><span class="mord text"><span class="mord">Output</span></span></span></span></span><span class="vlist-s">​</span></span><span class="vlist-r"><span class="vlist"><span></span></span></span></span></span><span class="mclose nulldelimiter"></span></span><span class="mspace"></span><span class="mbin">×</span><span class="mspace"></span></span><span class="base"><span class="strut"></span><span class="mord">100%</span></span></span></span></span>
<hr data-start="5196" data-end="5199">
<h1 data-section-id="1nk1iot" data-start="5201" data-end="5218">10. AC Machines</h1>
<p data-start="5220" data-end="5274">AC motors are analyzed using speed and slip equations.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="9eteno" data-start="5276" data-end="5297">Synchronous Speed</h3>
<span class="katex-display"><span class="katex"><span class="katex-mathml"><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="block"><semantics><mrow><msub><mi>N</mi><mi>s</mi></msub><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>120</mn><mi>f</mi></mrow><mi>P</mi></mfrac></mrow><annotation encoding="application/x-tex">N_s
=
\frac{120f}{P}</annotation></semantics></math></span><span class="katex-html" aria-hidden="true"><span class="base"><span class="strut"></span><span class="mord"><span class="mord mathnormal">N</span><span class="msupsub"><span class="vlist-t vlist-t2"><span class="vlist-r"><span class="vlist"><span><span class="pstrut"></span><span class="sizing reset-size6 size3 mtight"><span class="mord mathnormal mtight">s</span></span></span></span><span class="vlist-s">​</span></span><span class="vlist-r"><span class="vlist"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span class="mspace"></span><span class="mrel">=</span><span class="mspace"></span></span><span class="base"><span class="strut"></span><span class="mord"><span class="mopen nulldelimiter"></span><span class="mfrac"><span class="vlist-t vlist-t2"><span class="vlist-r"><span class="vlist"><span><span class="pstrut"></span><span class="mord"><span class="mord mathnormal">P</span></span></span><span><span class="pstrut"></span><span class="frac-line"></span></span><span><span class="pstrut"></span><span class="mord"><span class="mord">120</span><span class="mord mathnormal">f</span></span></span></span><span class="vlist-s">​</span></span><span class="vlist-r"><span class="vlist"><span></span></span></span></span></span><span class="mclose nulldelimiter"></span></span></span></span></span></span>
<h3 data-section-id="ynopwg" data-start="5327" data-end="5335">Slip</h3>
<span class="katex-display"><span class="katex"><span class="katex-mathml"><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="block"><semantics><mrow><mi>s</mi><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><msub><mi>N</mi><mi>s</mi></msub><mo>−</mo><msub><mi>N</mi><mi>r</mi></msub></mrow><msub><mi>N</mi><mi>s</mi></msub></mfrac></mrow><annotation encoding="application/x-tex">s
=
\frac{N_s-N_r}
{N_s}</annotation></semantics></math></span><span class="katex-html" aria-hidden="true"><span class="base"><span class="strut"></span><span class="mord mathnormal">s</span><span <span class="katex-display"><span class="katex"><span class="katex-mathml"><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="block"><semantics><mrow><mi>S</mi><mi>I</mi><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mi>P</mi><mi>R</mi><mi>T</mi></mrow><mn>100</mn></mfrac></mrow><annotation encoding="application/x-tex">SI

<h3 data-section-id="1f6tva9" data-start="6031" data-end="6052">Compound Interest</h3>
<span class="katex-display"><span class="katex"><span class="katex-mathml"><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="block"><semantics><mrow><mi>A</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>P</mi><msup><mrow><mo fence="true">(</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mi>r</mi><mi>n</mi></mfrac><mo fence="true">)</mo></mrow><mrow><mi>n</mi><mi>t</mi></mrow></msup></mrow><annotation encoding="application/x-tex">A
=
P
\left(
1+\frac{r}{n}
\right)^{nt}</annotation></semantics></math></span><span class="katex-html" aria-hidden="true"><span class="base"><span class="strut"></span><span class="mord mathnormal">A</span><span class="mspace"></span><span class="mrel">=</span><span class="mspace"></span></span><span class="base"><span class="strut"></span><span class="mord mathnormal">P</span><span class="mspace"></span><span class="minner"><span class="minner"><span class="mopen delimcenter"><span class="delimsizing size2">(</span></span><span class="mord">1</span><span class="mspace"></span><span class="mbin">+</span><span class="mspace"></span><span class="mord"><span class="mopen nulldelimiter"></span><span class="mfrac"><span class="vlist-t vlist-t2"><span class="vlist-r"><span class="vlist"><span><span class="pstrut"></span><span class="mord"><span class="mord mathnormal">n</span></span></span><span><span class="pstrut"></span><span class="frac-line"></span></span><span><span class="pstrut"></span><span class="mord"><span class="mord mathnormal">r</span></span></span></span><span class="vlist-s">​</span></span><span class="vlist-r"><span class="vlist"><span></span></span></span></span></span><span class="mclose nulldelimiter"></span></span><span class="mclose delimcenter"><span class="delimsizing size2">)</span></span></span><span class="msupsub"><span class="vlist-t"><span class="vlist-r"><span class="vlist"><span><span class="pstrut"></span><span class="sizing reset-size6 size3 mtight"><span class="mord mtight"><span class="mord mathnormal mtight">n</span><span class="mord mathnormal mtight">t</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span>
<h3 data-section-id="rbegji" data-start="6101" data-end="6115">Percentage</h3>
<span class="katex-display"><span class="katex"><span class="katex-mathml"><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="block"><semantics><mrow><mtext>Percentage</mtext><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mtext>Obtained&nbsp;Marks</mtext><mtext>Total&nbsp;Marks</mtext></mfrac><mo>×</mo><mn>100</mn></mrow><annotation encoding="application/x-tex">\text{Percentage}
=
\frac{\text{Obtained Marks}}
{\text{Total Marks}}
\times100</annotation></semantics></math></span><span class="katex-html" aria-hidden="true"><span class="base"><span class="strut"></span><span class="mord text"><span class="mord">Percentage</span></span><span class="mspace"></span><span class="mrel">=</span><span class="mspace"></span></span><span class="base"><span class="strut"></span><span class="mord"><span class="mopen nulldelimiter"></span><span class="mfrac"><span class="vlist-t vlist-t2"><span class="vlist-r"><span class="vlist"><span><span class="pstrut"></span><span class="mord"><span class="mord text"><span class="mord">Total&nbsp;Marks</span></span></span></span><span><span class="pstrut"></span><span class="frac-line"></span></span><span><span class="pstrut"></span><span class="mord"><span class="mord text"><span class="mord">Obtained&nbsp;Marks</span></span></span></span></span><span class="vlist-s">​</span></span><span class="vlist-r"><span class="vlist"><span></span></span></span></span></span><span class="mclose nulldelimiter"></span></span><span class="mspace"></span><span class="mbin">×</span><span class="mspace"></span></span><span class="base"><span class="strut"></span><span class="mord">100</span></span></span></span></span>
<h3 data-section-id="19w1kx1" data-start="6204" data-end="6215">Average</h3>
<span class="katex-display"><span class="katex"><span class="katex-mathml"><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="block"><semantics><mrow><mtext>Average</mtext><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mtext>Sum&nbsp;of&nbsp;Values</mtext><mtext>Number&nbsp;of&nbsp;Values</mtext></mfrac></mrow><annotation encoding="application/x-tex">\text{Average}
=
\frac{\text{Sum of Values}}
{\text{Number of Values}}</annotation></semantics></math></span><span class="katex-html" aria-hidden="true"><span class="base"><span class="strut"></span><span class="mord text"><span class="mord">Average</span></span><span class="mspace"></span><span class="mrel">=</span><span class="mspace"></span></span><span class="base"><span class="strut"></span><span class="mord"><span class="mopen nulldelimiter"></span><span class="mfrac"><span class="vlist-t vlist-t2"><span class="vlist-r"><span class="vlist"><span><span class="pstrut"></span><span class="mord"><span class="mord text"><span class="mord">Number&nbsp;of&nbsp;Values</span></span></span></span><span><span class="pstrut"></span><span class="frac-line"></span></span><span><span class="pstrut"></span><span class="mord"><span class="mord text"><span class="mord">Sum&nbsp;of&nbsp;Values</span></span></span></span></span><span class="vlist-s">​</span></span><span class="vlist-r"><span class="vlist"><span></span></span></span></span></span><span class="mclose nulldelimiter"></span></span></span></span></span></span>
<hr data-start="6295" data-end="6298">
<h1 data-section-id="fsb6xx" data-start="6300" data-end="6312">Conclusion</h1>
<p data-start="6314" data-end="7056" data-is-last-node="" data-is-only-node="">Mathematical formulas are indispensable across engineering, computer science, artificial intelligence, networking, statistics, physics, and mathematics. They provide standardized methods for analyzing systems, optimizing algorithms, measuring performance, and solving complex problems. From evaluating CPU scheduling in operating systems and calculating network delays to training machine learning models and analyzing electrical circuits, these equations serve as the fundamental language of science and technology. A strong understanding of these formulas enables engineers, researchers, and students to design efficient systems, interpret experimental results, and develop innovative solutions across a wide range of technical disciplines.</p><!--EndFragment-->
</body>
</html>

TRANSFORMED:

<!--StartFragment--><h1 data-section-id="xgsgjz" data-start="758" data-end="805" class="PDq2pG_selectionAnchorContainer">1. Software Engineering and Operating Systems<span aria-hidden="true" class="PDq2pG_selectionAnchor"></span></h1>
<p data-start="807" data-end="949">Software engineering and operating systems rely on performance metrics to evaluate process scheduling, CPU utilization, and system efficiency.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="owkoul" data-start="951" data-end="970">Turnaround Time</h3>
<p data-start="972" data-end="1087">Turnaround time represents the total time taken for a process to complete execution after it arrives in the system.</p>
<span data-latex="\text{Turnaround Time} = \text{Completion Time} - \text{Arrival Time}"></span>
<h3 data-section-id="tdya2g" data-start="1166" data-end="1182">Waiting Time</h3>
<p data-start="1184" data-end="1280">Waiting time is the total duration a process spends waiting in the ready queue before execution.</p>
<span data-latex="\text{Waiting Time} = \text{Turnaround Time} - \text{Burst Time}"></span>
<h3 data-section-id="12rlxzk" data-start="1354" data-end="1371" class="">Response Time</h3>
<p data-start="1373" data-end="1456">Response time measures how quickly a process receives CPU attention after arriving.</p>
<span data-latex="\text{Response Time} = \text{First Response Time} - \text{Arrival Time}"></span>
<h3 data-section-id="vp1wy2" data-start="1537" data-end="1556" class="">CPU Utilization</h3>
<p data-start="1558" data-end="1628">CPU utilization indicates how efficiently the processor is being used.</p>
<span data-latex="\text{CPU Utilization}
=
\frac{\text{CPU Busy Time}}
{\text{Total Time}}
\times100\%"></span>
<h3 data-section-id="18mgb1o" data-start="1722" data-end="1736" class="">Throughput</h3>
<p data-start="1738" data-end="1810">Throughput measures the number of processes completed in a unit of time.</p>
<span data-latex="\text{Throughput}
=
\frac{\text{Number of Processes}}
{\text{Total Time}}"></span>
<h3 data-section-id="h9jim2" data-start="1893" data-end="1918" class="">Effective Access Time</h3>
<p data-start="1920" data-end="1998">This formula estimates the average memory access time considering page faults.</p>
<span data-latex="EAT
=
(1-p)m+pP"></span>
<p data-start="2023" data-end="2029">where:</p>
<ul data-start="2031" data-end="2126">
<li data-section-id="rvilmx" data-start="2031" data-end="2059">
<span data-latex="m"></span> = memory access time
</li>
<li data-section-id="12l695h" data-start="2060" data-end="2093">
<span data-latex="P"></span> = page fault service time
</li>
<li data-section-id="kuat2o" data-start="2094" data-end="2126">
<span data-latex="p"></span> = page fault probability
</li>
</ul>
<h3 data-section-id="yfiock" data-start="2128" data-end="2139">Speedup</h3>
<p data-start="2141" data-end="2207">Speedup compares the execution time before and after optimization.</p>
<span data-latex="S
=
\frac{\text{Old Execution Time}}
{\text{New Execution Time}}"></span>

<h1 data-section-id="1bpli4w" data-start="2286" data-end="2308">2. Computer Networks</h1>
<p data-start="2310" data-end="2415">Computer networks use mathematical formulas to evaluate communication delays and transmission efficiency.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="12s4qjp" data-start="2417" data-end="2439">Transmission Delay</h3>
<span data-latex="T_t
=
\frac{L}{R}"></span>
<p data-start="2466" data-end="2472">where:</p>
<ul data-start="2474" data-end="2523">
<li data-section-id="oacs45" data-start="2474" data-end="2495">
<span data-latex="L"></span> = packet size
</li>
<li data-section-id="1expp76" data-start="2496" data-end="2523">
<span data-latex="R"></span> = transmission rate
</li>
</ul>
<h3 data-section-id="1xftzlh" data-start="2525" data-end="2546">Propagation Delay</h3>
<span data-latex="T_p
=
\frac{d}{v}"></span>
<p data-start="2573" data-end="2579">where:</p>
<ul data-start="2581" data-end="2640">
<li data-section-id="3uq551" data-start="2581" data-end="2612">
<span data-latex="d"></span> = transmission distance
</li>
<li data-section-id="138e09v" data-start="2613" data-end="2640">
<span data-latex="v"></span> = propagation speed
</li>
</ul>
<h3 data-section-id="18dbxu7" data-start="2642" data-end="2665">Total Network Delay</h3>
<span data-latex="D
=
T_t
+
T_p
+
T_q
+
T_{proc}"></span>
<p data-start="2705" data-end="2711">where:</p>
<ul data-start="2713" data-end="2773">
<li data-section-id="r80uei" data-start="2713" data-end="2739">
<span data-latex="T_q"></span> = queueing delay
</li>
<li data-section-id="1s7dmbt" data-start="2740" data-end="2773">
<span data-latex="T_{proc}"></span> = processing delay
</li>
</ul>
<h3 data-section-id="djrq9v" data-start="2775" data-end="2800">Bandwidth Utilization</h3>
<span data-latex="U
=
\frac{\text{Useful Data}}
{\text{Total Bandwidth}}"></span>

<h1 data-section-id="2idg14" data-start="2869" data-end="2890">3. Database Systems</h1>
<p data-start="2892" data-end="2971">Database systems use disk performance formulas to estimate data retrieval time.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="mfp7eq" data-start="2973" data-end="2993">Disk Access Time</h3>
<span data-latex="T
=
T_s
+
T_r
+
T_t"></span>
<p data-start="3022" data-end="3028">where:</p>
<ul data-start="3030" data-end="3108">
<li data-section-id="1pg3kch" data-start="3030" data-end="3051">
<span data-latex="T_s"></span> = seek time
</li>
<li data-section-id="1ijm78c" data-start="3052" data-end="3082">
<span data-latex="T_r"></span> = rotational latency
</li>
<li data-section-id="na12qd" data-start="3083" data-end="3108">
<span data-latex="T_t"></span> = transfer time
</li>
</ul>

<h1 data-section-id="s3ggx1" data-start="3115" data-end="3136">4. Software Testing</h1>
<p data-start="3138" data-end="3220">Software testing metrics help evaluate software quality and testing effectiveness.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="awm3fv" data-start="3222" data-end="3240">Defect Density</h3>
<span data-latex="\text{Defect Density}
=
\frac{\text{Number of Defects}}
{\text{KLOC}}"></span>
<h3 data-section-id="16ftr8e" data-start="3319" data-end="3336">Test Coverage</h3>
<span data-latex="\text{Coverage}
=
\frac{\text{Executed Statements}}
{\text{Total Statements}}
\times100\%"></span>
<h3 data-section-id="6v7e96" data-start="3435" data-end="3464">Defect Removal Efficiency</h3>
<span data-latex="DRE
=
\frac{\text{Defects Removed}}
{\text{Total Defects}}
\times100\%"></span>

<h1 data-section-id="ypy6jw" data-start="3549" data-end="3569">5. Compiler Design</h1>
<p data-start="3571" data-end="3631">Compiler optimization often measures compression efficiency.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="dtop9l" data-start="3633" data-end="3654">Compression Ratio</h3>
<span data-latex="CR
=
\frac{\text{Original Size}}
{\text{Compressed Size}}"></span>

<h1 data-section-id="oqoyvh" data-start="3726" data-end="3754">6. Artificial Intelligence</h1>
<p data-start="3756" data-end="3868">Artificial intelligence algorithms heavily depend on mathematical activation functions and optimization methods.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="1qwcw46" data-start="3870" data-end="3890">Sigmoid Function</h3>
<span data-latex="\sigma(x)
=
\frac1{1+e^{-x}}"></span>
<h3 data-section-id="e23zz2" data-start="3928" data-end="3956">ReLU Activation Function</h3>
<span data-latex="f(x)
=
\max(0,x)"></span>
<h3 data-section-id="1b7f1ku" data-start="3982" data-end="4002">Softmax Function</h3>
<span data-latex="P_i
=
\frac{e^{x_i}}
{\sum_j e^{x_j}}"></span>
<h3 data-section-id="4wvzb0" data-start="4049" data-end="4071">Mean Squared Error</h3>
<span data-latex="MSE
=
\frac1n
\sum_{i=1}^{n}
(y_i-\hat y_i)^2"></span>
<h3 data-section-id="1sfwob4" data-start="4126" data-end="4148">Cross Entropy Loss</h3>
<span data-latex="L
=
-\sum_i
y_i
\log(\hat y_i)"></span>
<h3 data-section-id="1vjk2y6" data-start="4188" data-end="4208">Gradient Descent</h3>
<span data-latex="\theta
=
\theta
-
\alpha
\nabla J(\theta)"></span>

<h1 data-section-id="19vmoca" data-start="4264" data-end="4279">7. Statistics</h1>
<p data-start="4281" data-end="4353">Statistics provides tools for analyzing and interpreting numerical data.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="ynk17l" data-start="4355" data-end="4363">Mean</h3>
<span data-latex="\mu
=
\frac1n
\sum_{i=1}^{n}
x_i"></span>
<h3 data-section-id="7rghsz" data-start="4405" data-end="4417">Variance</h3>
<span data-latex="\sigma^2
=
\frac1n
\sum_{i=1}^{n}
(x_i-\mu)^2"></span>
<h3 data-section-id="17ez3em" data-start="4472" data-end="4494">Standard Deviation</h3>
<span data-latex="\sigma
=
\sqrt{\sigma^2}"></span>
<h3 data-section-id="74ka2z" data-start="4528" data-end="4543">Probability</h3>
<span data-latex="P(A)
=
\frac{\text{Favorable Outcomes}}
{\text{Total Outcomes}}"></span>

<h1 data-section-id="1pg5kt7" data-start="4621" data-end="4645">8. Digital Electronics</h1>
<p data-start="4647" data-end="4715">Digital electronics relies on binary arithmetic and Boolean algebra.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="nj9mgx" data-start="4717" data-end="4749">Binary to Decimal Conversion</h3>
<span data-latex="N
=
\sum_i
b_i2^i"></span>
<h3 data-section-id="1711nzm" data-start="4776" data-end="4798">Boolean Identities</h3>
<span data-latex="A+A=A"></span>
<span data-latex="A\cdot1=A"></span>
<span data-latex="A+0=A"></span>

<h1 data-section-id="19du892" data-start="4848" data-end="4875" class="">9. Electrical Engineering</h1>
<p data-start="4877" data-end="4980">Electrical engineering formulas describe relationships between voltage, current, resistance, and power.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="a2xjmq" data-start="4982" data-end="4995">Ohm's Law</h3>
<span data-latex="V
=
IR"></span>
<h3 data-section-id="1iiu0ai" data-start="5011" data-end="5029">Electric Power</h3>
<span data-latex="P
=
VI"></span>
<h3 data-section-id="13blzq3" data-start="5045" data-end="5057">AC Power</h3>
<span data-latex="P
=
VI\cos\phi"></span>
<h3 data-section-id="17f113e" data-start="5081" data-end="5102">Electrical Energy</h3>
<span data-latex="E
=
Pt"></span>
<h3 data-section-id="31g7e9" data-start="5118" data-end="5132">Efficiency</h3>
<span data-latex="\eta
=
\frac{\text{Output}}
{\text{Input}}
\times100\%"></span>

<h1 data-section-id="1nk1iot" data-start="5201" data-end="5218">10. AC Machines</h1>
<p data-start="5220" data-end="5274">AC motors are analyzed using speed and slip equations.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="9eteno" data-start="5276" data-end="5297">Synchronous Speed</h3>
<span data-latex="N_s
=
\frac{120f}{P}"></span>
<h3 data-section-id="ynopwg" data-start="5327" data-end="5335">Slip</h3>
<span data-latex="s
=
\frac{N_s-N_r}
{N_s}"></span>
<h3 data-section-id="1iu2n2u" data-start="5369" data-end="5388">Rotor Frequency</h3>
<span data-latex="f_r
=
sf"></span>

<h1 data-section-id="1xpfi0z" data-start="5411" data-end="5424">11. Physics</h1>
<p data-start="5426" data-end="5479">Physics equations describe motion, force, and energy.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="olmf5d" data-start="5481" data-end="5504">Newton's Second Law</h3>
<span data-latex="F
=
ma"></span>
<h3 data-section-id="51ly5v" data-start="5520" data-end="5538">Kinetic Energy</h3>
<span data-latex="KE
=
\frac12mv^2"></span>
<h3 data-section-id="oar9qs" data-start="5564" data-end="5584">Potential Energy</h3>
<span data-latex="PE
=
mgh"></span>
<h3 data-section-id="xeoaj2" data-start="5602" data-end="5614">Momentum</h3>
<span data-latex="p
=
mv"></span>
<h3 data-section-id="1dhnfry" data-start="5630" data-end="5641">Density</h3>
<span data-latex="\rho
=
\frac{m}{V}"></span>

<h1 data-section-id="1c8rh7j" data-start="5674" data-end="5691">12. Mathematics</h1>
<p data-start="5693" data-end="5760">Mathematics provides the foundation for all scientific disciplines.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="y3kkkr" data-start="5762" data-end="5782">Distance Formula</h3>
<span data-latex="d
=
\sqrt{(x_2-x_1)^2+(y_2-y_1)^2}"></span>
<h3 data-section-id="1usjbd0" data-start="5826" data-end="5847">Quadratic Formula</h3>
<span data-latex="x
=
\frac{-b\pm\sqrt{b^2-4ac}}
{2a}"></span>
<h3 data-section-id="f6qf9n" data-start="5892" data-end="5912">Area of a Circle</h3>
<span data-latex="A
=
\pi r^2"></span>
<h3 data-section-id="1k69sv7" data-start="5933" data-end="5962" class="">Circumference of a Circle</h3>
<span data-latex="C
=
2\pi r"></span>
<h3 data-section-id="mfnsz2" data-start="5982" data-end="6001">Simple Interest</h3>
<span data-latex="SI
=
\frac{PRT}{100}"></span>
<h3 data-section-id="1f6tva9" data-start="6031" data-end="6052">Compound Interest</h3>
<span data-latex="A
=
P
\left(
1+\frac{r}{n}
\right)^{nt}"></span>
<h3 data-section-id="rbegji" data-start="6101" data-end="6115">Percentage</h3>
<span data-latex="\text{Percentage}
=
\frac{\text{Obtained Marks}}
{\text{Total Marks}}
\times100"></span>
<h3 data-section-id="19w1kx1" data-start="6204" data-end="6215">Average</h3>
<span data-latex="\text{Average}
=
\frac{\text{Sum of Values}}
{\text{Number of Values}}"></span>

<h1 data-section-id="fsb6xx" data-start="6300" data-end="6312">Conclusion</h1>
<p data-start="6314" data-end="7056" data-is-last-node="" data-is-only-node="">Mathematical formulas are indispensable across engineering, computer science, artificial intelligence, networking, statistics, physics, and mathematics. They provide standardized methods for analyzing systems, optimizing algorithms, measuring performance, and solving complex problems. From evaluating CPU scheduling in operating systems and calculating network delays to training machine learning models and analyzing electrical circuits, these equations serve as the fundamental language of science and technology. A strong understanding of these formulas enables engineers, researchers, and students to design efficient systems, interpret experimental results, and develop innovative solutions across a wide range of technical disciplines.</p><!--EndFragment-->

<body>
<!--StartFragment--><html><head></head><body><h1><strong>Engineering, Computer Science, and Mathematics Formula Handbook</strong></h1><h2>Introduction</h2><p>Mathematical formulas play a fundamental role in engineering, computer science, artificial intelligence, physics, and statistics. They provide precise methods for solving problems, analyzing systems, evaluating performance, and designing efficient algorithms. Whether calculating the execution time of a computer process, determining the efficiency of an electrical machine, training a neural network, or measuring statistical variation, mathematical equations form the backbone of scientific and technological advancements. This report presents a collection of commonly used formulas from various disciplines along with a brief explanation of their significance.</p><hr><h1>1. Software Engineering and Operating Systems</h1><p>Software engineering and operating systems rely on performance metrics to evaluate process scheduling, CPU utilization, and system efficiency.</p><h3>Turnaround Time</h3><p>Turnaround time represents the total time taken for a process to complete execution after it arrives in the system.</p><p>[<br>\text{Turnaround Time} = \text{Completion Time} - \text{Arrival Time}<br>]</p><h3>Waiting Time</h3><p>Waiting time is the total duration a process spends waiting in the ready queue before execution.</p><p>[<br>\text{Waiting Time} = \text{Turnaround Time} - \text{Burst Time}<br>]</p><h3>Response Time</h3><p>Response time measures how quickly a process receives CPU attention after arriving.</p><p>[<br>\text{Response Time} = \text{First Response Time} - \text{Arrival Time}<br>]</p><h3>CPU Utilization</h3><p>CPU utilization indicates how efficiently the processor is being used.</p><h1>[<br>\text{CPU Utilization}</h1><p>\frac{\text{CPU Busy Time}}<br>{\text{Total Time}}<br>\times100%<br>]</p><h3>Throughput</h3><p>Throughput measures the number of processes completed in a unit of time.</p><h1>[<br>\text{Throughput}</h1><p>\frac{\text{Number of Processes}}<br>{\text{Total Time}}<br>]</p><h3>Effective Access Time</h3><p>This formula estimates the average memory access time considering page faults.</p><h1>[<br>EAT</h1><p>(1-p)m+pP<br>]</p><p>where:</p><ul><li><p>(m) = memory access time</p></li><li><p>(P) = page fault service time</p></li><li><p>(p) = page fault probability</p></li></ul><h3>Speedup</h3><p>Speedup compares the execution time before and after optimization.</p><h1>[<br>S</h1><p>\frac{\text{Old Execution Time}}<br>{\text{New Execution Time}}<br>]</p><hr><h1>2. Computer Networks</h1><p>Computer networks use mathematical formulas to evaluate communication delays and transmission efficiency.</p><h3>Transmission Delay</h3><h1>[<br>T_t</h1><p>\frac{L}{R}<br>]</p><p>where:</p><ul><li><p>(L) = packet size</p></li><li><p>(R) = transmission rate</p></li></ul><h3>Propagation Delay</h3><h1>[<br>T_p</h1><p>\frac{d}{v}<br>]</p><p>where:</p><ul><li><p>(d) = transmission distance</p></li><li><p>(v) = propagation speed</p></li></ul><h3>Total Network Delay</h3><h1>[<br>D</h1><p>T_t<br>+<br>T_p<br>+<br>T_q<br>+<br>T_{proc}<br>]</p><p>where:</p><ul><li><p>(T_q) = queueing delay</p></li><li><p>(T_{proc}) = processing delay</p></li></ul><h3>Bandwidth Utilization</h3><h1>[<br>U</h1><p>\frac{\text{Useful Data}}<br>{\text{Total Bandwidth}}<br>]</p><hr><h1>3. Database Systems</h1><p>Database systems use disk performance formulas to estimate data retrieval time.</p><h3>Disk Access Time</h3><h1>[<br>T</h1><p>T_s<br>+<br>T_r<br>+<br>T_t<br>]</p><p>where:</p><ul><li><p>(T_s) = seek time</p></li><li><p>(T_r) = rotational latency</p></li><li><p>(T_t) = transfer time</p></li></ul><hr><h1>4. Software Testing</h1><p>Software testing metrics help evaluate software quality and testing effectiveness.</p><h3>Defect Density</h3><h1>[<br>\text{Defect Density}</h1><p>\frac{\text{Number of Defects}}<br>{\text{KLOC}}<br>]</p><h3>Test Coverage</h3><h1>[<br>\text{Coverage}</h1><p>\frac{\text{Executed Statements}}<br>{\text{Total Statements}}<br>\times100%<br>]</p><h3>Defect Removal Efficiency</h3><h1>[<br>DRE</h1><p>\frac{\text{Defects Removed}}<br>{\text{Total Defects}}<br>\times100%<br>]</p><hr><h1>5. Compiler Design</h1><p>Compiler optimization often measures compression efficiency.</p><h3>Compression Ratio</h3><h1>[<br>CR</h1><p>\frac{\text{Original Size}}<br>{\text{Compressed Size}}<br>]</p><hr><h1>6. Artificial Intelligence</h1><p>Artificial intelligence algorithms heavily depend on mathematical activation functions and optimization methods.</p><h3>Sigmoid Function</h3><h1>[<br>\sigma(x)</h1><p>\frac1{1+e^{-x}}<br>]</p><h3>ReLU Activation Function</h3><h1>[<br>f(x)</h1><p>\max(0,x)<br>]</p><h3>Softmax Function</h3><h1>[<br>P_i</h1><p>\frac{e^{x_i}}<br>{\sum_j e^{x_j}}<br>]</p><h3>Mean Squared Error</h3><h1>[<br>MSE</h1><p>\frac1n<br>\sum_{i=1}^{n}<br>(y_i-\hat y_i)^2<br>]</p><h3>Cross Entropy Loss</h3><h1>[<br>L</h1><p>-\sum_i<br>y_i<br>\log(\hat y_i)<br>]</p><h3>Gradient Descent</h3><h1>[<br>\theta</h1><h2>\theta</h2><p>\alpha<br>\nabla J(\theta)<br>]</p><hr><h1>7. Statistics</h1><p>Statistics provides tools for analyzing and interpreting numerical data.</p><h3>Mean</h3><h1>[<br>\mu</h1><p>\frac1n<br>\sum_{i=1}^{n}<br>x_i<br>]</p><h3>Variance</h3><h1>[<br>\sigma^2</h1><p>\frac1n<br>\sum_{i=1}^{n}<br>(x_i-\mu)^2<br>]</p><h3>Standard Deviation</h3><h1>[<br>\sigma</h1><p>\sqrt{\sigma^2}<br>]</p><h3>Probability</h3><h1>[<br>P(A)</h1><p>\frac{\text{Favorable Outcomes}}<br>{\text{Total Outcomes}}<br>]</p><hr><h1>8. Digital Electronics</h1><p>Digital electronics relies on binary arithmetic and Boolean algebra.</p><h3>Binary to Decimal Conversion</h3><h1>[<br>N</h1><p>\sum_i<br>b_i2^i<br>]</p><h3>Boolean Identities</h3><p>[<br>A+A=A<br>]</p><p>[<br>A\cdot1=A<br>]</p><p>[<br>A+0=A<br>]</p><hr><h1>9. Electrical Engineering</h1><p>Electrical engineering formulas describe relationships between voltage, current, resistance, and power.</p><h3>Ohm's Law</h3><h1>[<br>V</h1><p>IR<br>]</p><h3>Electric Power</h3><h1>[<br>P</h1><p>VI<br>]</p><h3>AC Power</h3><h1>[<br>P</h1><p>VI\cos\phi<br>]</p><h3>Electrical Energy</h3><h1>[<br>E</h1><p>Pt<br>]</p><h3>Efficiency</h3><h1>[<br>\eta</h1><p>\frac{\text{Output}}<br>{\text{Input}}<br>\times100%<br>]</p><hr><h1>10. AC Machines</h1><p>AC motors are analyzed using speed and slip equations.</p><h3>Synchronous Speed</h3><h1>[<br>N_s</h1><p>\frac{120f}{P}<br>]</p><h3>Slip</h3><h1>[<br>s</h1><p>\frac{N_s-N_r}<br>{N_s}<br>]</p><h3>Rotor Frequency</h3><h1>[<br>f_r</h1><p>sf<br>]</p><hr><h1>11. Physics</h1><p>Physics equations describe motion, force, and energy.</p><h3>Newton's Second Law</h3><h1>[<br>F</h1><p>ma<br>]</p><h3>Kinetic Energy</h3><h1>[<br>KE</h1><p>\frac12mv^2<br>]</p><h3>Potential Energy</h3><h1>[<br>PE</h1><p>mgh<br>]</p><h3>Momentum</h3><h1>[<br>p</h1><p>mv<br>]</p><h3>Density</h3><h1>[<br>\rho</h1><p>\frac{m}{V}<br>]</p><hr><h1>12. Mathematics</h1><p>Mathematics provides the foundation for all scientific disciplines.</p><h3>Distance Formula</h3><h1>[<br>d</h1><p>\sqrt{(x_2-x_1)^2+(y_2-y_1)^2}<br>]</p><h3>Quadratic Formula</h3><h1>[<br>x</h1><p>\frac{-b\pm\sqrt{b^2-4ac}}<br>{2a}<br>]</p><h3>Area of a Circle</h3><h1>[<br>A</h1><p>\pi r^2<br>]</p><h3>Circumference of a Circle</h3><h1>[<br>C</h1><p>2\pi r<br>]</p><h3>Simple Interest</h3><h1>[<br>SI</h1><p>\frac{PRT}{100}<br>]</p><h3>Compound Interest</h3><h1>[<br>A</h1><p>P<br>\left(<br>1+\frac{r}{n}<br>\right)^{nt}<br>]</p><h3>Percentage</h3><h1>[<br>\text{Percentage}</h1><p>\frac{\text{Obtained Marks}}<br>{\text{Total Marks}}<br>\times100<br>]</p><h3>Average</h3><h1>[<br>\text{Average}</h1><p>\frac{\text{Sum of Values}}<br>{\text{Number of Values}}<br>]</p><hr><h1>Conclusion</h1><p>Mathematical formulas are indispensable across engineering, computer science, artificial intelligence, networking, statistics, physics, and mathematics. They provide standardized methods for analyzing systems, optimizing algorithms, measuring performance, and solving complex problems. From evaluating CPU scheduling in operating systems and calculating network delays to training machine learning models and analyzing electrical circuits, these equations serve as the fundamental language of science and technology. A strong understanding of these formulas enables engineers, researchers, and students to design efficient systems, interpret experimental results, and develop innovative solutions across a wide range of technical disciplines.</p></body></html><!--EndFragment-->
</body>
</html>

<!--StartFragment--><h1><strong>Engineering, Computer Science, and Mathematics Formula Handbook</strong></h1><h2>Introduction</h2><p>Mathematical formulas play a fundamental role in engineering, computer science, artificial intelligence, physics, and statistics. They provide precise methods for solving problems, analyzing systems, evaluating performance, and designing efficient algorithms. Whether calculating the execution time of a computer process, determining the efficiency of an electrical machine, training a neural network, or measuring statistical variation, mathematical equations form the backbone of scientific and technological advancements. This report presents a collection of commonly used formulas from various disciplines along with a brief explanation of their significance.</p><h1>1. Compiler Design</h1><p>Compiler optimization often measures compression efficiency.</p><h3>Compression Ratio</h3><h1>[<br>CR</h1><p>\frac{\text{Original Size}}<br>{\text{Compressed Size}}<br>]</p><hr><h1>6. Artificial Intelligence</h1><p>Artificial intelligence algorithms heavily depend on mathematical activation functions and optimization methods.</p><h3>Sigmoid Function</h3><h1>[<br>\sigma(x)</h1><p>\frac1{1+e^{-x}}<br>]</p><h3>ReLU Activation Function</h3><h1>[<br>f(x)</h1><p>\max(0,x)<br>]</p><h3>Softmax Function</h3><h1>[<br>P_i</h1><p>\frac{e^{x_i}}<br>{\sum_j e^{x_j}}<br>]</p><h3>Mean Squared Error</h3><h1>[<br>MSE</h1><p>\frac1n<br>\sum_{i=1}^{n}<br>(y_i-\hat y_i)^2<br>]</p><h3>Cross Entropy Loss</h3><h1>[<br>L</h1><p>-\sum_i<br>y_i<br>\log(\hat y_i)<br>]</p><h3>Gradient Descent</h3><h1>[<br>\theta</h1><h2>\theta</h2><p>\alpha<br>\nabla J(\theta)<br>]</p><hr><h1>7. Statistics</h1><p>Statistics provides tools for analyzing and interpreting numerical data.</p><h3>Mean</h3><h1>[<br>\mu</h1><p>\frac1n<br>\sum_{i=1}^{n}<br>x_i<br>]</p><h3>Variance</h3><h1>[<br>\sigma^2</h1><p>\frac1n<br>\sum_{i=1}^{n}<br>(x_i-\mu)^2<br>]</p><h3>Standard Deviation</h3><h1>[<br>\sigma</h1><p>\sqrt{\sigma^2}<br>]</p><h3>Probability</h3><h1>[<br>P(A)</h1><p>\frac{\text{Favorable Outcomes}}<br>{\text{Total Outcomes}}<br>]</p><hr><h1>8. Digital Electronics</h1><p>Digital electronics relies on binary arithmetic and Boolean algebra.</p><h3>Binary to Decimal Conversion</h3><h1>[<br>N</h1><p>\sum_i<br>b_i2^i<br>]</p><h3>Boolean Identities</h3><p>[<br>A+A=A<br>]</p><p>[<br>A\cdot1=A<br>]</p><p>[<br>A+0=A<br>]</p><hr><h1>9. Electrical Engineering</h1><p>Electrical engineering formulas describe relationships between voltage, current, resistance, and power.</p><h3>Ohm's Law</h3><h1>[<br>V</h1><p>IR<br>]</p><h3>Electric Power</h3><h1>[<br>P</h1><p>VI<br>]</p><h3>AC Power</h3><h1>[<br>P</h1><p>VI\cos\phi<br>]</p><h3>Electrical Energy</h3><h1>[<br>E</h1><p>Pt<br>]</p><h3>Efficiency</h3><h1>[<br>\eta</h1><p>\frac{\text{Output}}<br>{\text{Input}}<br>\times100%<br>]</p><hr><h1>10. AC Machines</h1><p>AC motors are analyzed using speed and slip equations.</p><h3>Synchronous Speed</h3><h1>[<br>N_s</h1><p>\frac{120f}{P}<br>]</p><h3>Slip</h3><h1>[<br>s</h1><p>\frac{N_s-N_r}<br>{N_s}<br>]</p><h3>Rotor Frequency</h3><h1>[<br>f_r</h1><p>sf<br>]</p><hr><h1>11. Physics</h1><p>Physics equations describe motion, force, and energy.</p><h3>Newton's Second Law</h3><h1>[<br>F</h1><p>ma<br>]</p><h3>Kinetic Energy</h3><h1>[<br>KE</h1><p>\frac12mv^2<br>]</p><h3>Potential Energy</h3><h1>[<br>PE</h1><p>mgh<br>]</p><h3>Momentum</h3><h1>[<br>p</h1><p>mv<br>]</p><h3>Density</h3><h1>[<br>\rho</h1><p>\frac{m}{V}<br>]</p><hr><h1>12. Mathematics</h1><p>Mathematics provides the foundation for all scientific disciplines.</p><h3>Distance Formula</h3><h1>[<br>d</h1><p>\sqrt{(x_2-x_1)^2+(y_2-y_1)^2}<br>]</p><h3>Quadratic Formula</h3><h1>[<br>x</h1><p>\frac{-b\pm\sqrt{b^2-4ac}}<br>{2a}<br>]</p><h3>Area of a Circle</h3><h1>[<br>A</h1><p>\pi r^2<br>]</p><h3>Circumference of a Circle</h3><h1>[<br>C</h1><p>2\pi r<br>]</p><h3>Simple Interest</h3><h1>[<br>SI</h1><p>\frac{PRT}{100}<br>]</p><h3>Compound Interest</h3><h1>[<br>A</h1><p>P<br>\left(<br>1+\frac{r}{n}<br>\right)^{nt}<br>]</p><h3>Percentage</h3><h1>[<br>\text{Percentage}</h1><p>\frac{\text{Obtained Marks}}<br>{\text{Total Marks}}<br>\times100<br>]</p><h3>Average</h3><h1>[<br>\text{Average}</h1><p>\frac{\text{Sum of Values}}<br>{\text{Number of Values}}<br>]</p><hr><h1>Conclusion</h1><p>Mathematical formulas are indispensable across engineering, computer science, artificial intelligence, networking, statistics, physics, and mathematics. They provide standardized methods for analyzing systems, optimizing algorithms, measuring performance, and solving complex problems. From evaluating CPU scheduling in operating systems and calculating network delays to training machine learning models and analyzing electrical circuits, these equations serve as the fundamental language of science and technology. A strong understanding of these formulas enables engineers, researchers, and students to design efficient systems, interpret experimental results, and develop innovative solutions across a wide range of technical disciplines.</p></body></html><!--EndFragment-->
</body>
</html>

<!--StartFragment--><h1><strong>Engineering, Computer Science, and Mathematics Formula Handbook</strong></h1><h2>Introduction</h2><p>Mathematical formulas play a fundamental role in engineering, computer science, artificial intelligence, physics, and statistics. They provide precise methods for solving problems, analyzing systems, evaluating performance, and designing efficient algorithms. Whether calculating the execution time of a computer process, determining the efficiency of an electrical machine, training a neural network, or measuring statistical variation, mathematical equations form the backbone of scientific and technological advancements. This report presents a collection of commonly used formulas from various disciplines along with a brief explanation of their significance.</p><h1>1. Software Engineering and Operating Systems</h1><p>Software engineering and operating systems rely on performance metrics to evaluate process scheduling, CPU utilization, and system efficiency.</p><h3>Turnaround Time</h3><p>Turnaround time represents the total time taken for a process to complete execution after it arrives in the system.</p><p>[<br>\text{Turnaround Time} = \text{Completion Time} - \text{Arrival Time}<br>]</p><h3>Waiting Time</h3><p>Waiting time is the total duration a process spends waiting in the ready queue before execution.</p><p>[<br>\text{Waiting Time} = \text{Turnaround Time} - \text{Burst Time}<br>]</p><h3>Response Time</h3><p>Response time measures how quickly a process receives CPU attention after arriving.</p><p>[<br>\text{Response Time} = \text{First Response Time} - \text{Arrival Time}<br>]</p><h3>CPU Utilization</h3><p>CPU utilization indicates how efficiently the processor is being used.</p><h1>[<br>\text{CPU Utilization}</h1><p>\frac{\text{CPU Busy Time}}<br>{\text{Total Time}}<br>\times100%<br>]</p><h3>Throughput</h3><p>Throughput measures the number of processes completed in a unit of time.</p><h1>[<br>\text{Throughput}</h1><p>\frac{\text{Number of Processes}}<br>{\text{Total Time}}<br>]</p><h3>Effective Access Time</h3><p>This formula estimates the average memory access time considering page faults.</p><h1>[<br>EAT</h1><p>(1-p)m+pP<br>]</p><p>where:</p><ul><li><p>(m) = memory access time</p></li><li><p>(P) = page fault service time</p></li><li><p>(p) = page fault probability</p></li></ul><h3>Speedup</h3><p>Speedup compares the execution time before and after optimization.</p><h1>[<br>S</h1><p>\frac{\text{Old Execution Time}}<br>{\text{New Execution Time}}<br>]</p><h1>2. Computer Networks</h1><p>Computer networks use mathematical formulas to evaluate communication delays and transmission efficiency.</p><h3>Transmission Delay</h3><h1>[<br>T_t</h1><p>\frac{L}{R}<br>]</p><p>where:</p><ul><li><p>(L) = packet size</p></li><li><p>(R) = transmission rate</p></li></ul><h3>Propagation Delay</h3><h1>[<br>T_p</h1><p>\frac{d}{v}<br>]</p><p>where:</p><ul><li><p>(d) = transmission distance</p></li><li><p>(v) = propagation speed</p></li></ul><h3>Total Network Delay</h3><h1>[<br>D</h1><p>T_t<br>+<br>T_p<br>+<br>T_q<br>+<br>T_{proc}<br>]</p><p>where:</p><ul><li><p>(T_q) = queueing delay</p></li><li><p>(T_{proc}) = processing delay</p></li></ul><h3>Bandwidth Utilization</h3><h1>[<br>U</h1><p>\frac{\text{Useful Data}}<br>{\text{Total Bandwidth}}<br>]</p><h1>3. Database Systems</h1><p>Database systems use disk performance formulas to estimate data retrieval time.</p><h3>Disk Access Time</h3><h1>[<br>T</h1><p>T_s<br>+<br>T_r<br>+<br>T_t<br>]</p><p>where:</p><ul><li><p>(T_s) = seek time</p></li><li><p>(T_r) = rotational latency</p></li><li><p>(T_t) = transfer time</p></li></ul><h1>4. Software Testing</h1><p>Software testing metrics help evaluate software quality and testing effectiveness.</p><h3>Defect Density</h3><h1>[<br>\text{Defect Density}</h1><p>\frac{\text{Number of Defects}}<br>{\text{KLOC}}<br>]</p><h3>Test Coverage</h3><h1>[<br>\text{Coverage}</h1><p>\frac{\text{Executed Statements}}<br>{\text{Total Statements}}<br>\times100%<br>]</p><h3>Defect Removal Efficiency</h3><h1>[<br>DRE</h1><p>\frac{\text{Defects Removed}}<br>{\text{Total Defects}}<br>\times100%<br>]</p><h1>5. Compiler Design</h1><p>Compiler optimization often measures compression efficiency.</p><h3>Compression Ratio</h3><h1>[<br>CR</h1><p>\frac{\text{Original Size}}<br>{\text{Compressed Size}}<br>]</p><h1>6. Artificial Intelligence</h1><p>Artificial intelligence algorithms heavily depend on mathematical activation functions and optimization methods.</p><h3>Sigmoid Function</h3><h1>[<br>\sigma(x)</h1><p>\frac1{1+e^{-x}}<br>]</p><h3>ReLU Activation Function</h3><h1>[<br>f(x)</h1><p>\max(0,x)<br>]</p><h3>Softmax Function</h3><h1>[<br>P_i</h1><p>\frac{e^{x_i}}<br>{\sum_j e^{x_j}}<br>]</p><h3>Mean Squared Error</h3><h1>[<br>MSE</h1><p>\frac1n<br>\sum_{i=1}^{n}<br>(y_i-\hat y_i)^2<br>]</p><h3>Cross Entropy Loss</h3><h1>[<br>L</h1><p>-\sum_i<br>y_i<br>\log(\hat y_i)<br>]</p><h3>Gradient Descent</h3><h1>[<br>\theta</h1><h2>\theta</h2><p>\alpha<br>\nabla J(\theta)<br>]</p><h1>7. Statistics</h1><p>Statistics provides tools for analyzing and interpreting numerical data.</p><h3>Mean</h3><h1>[<br>\mu</h1><p>\frac1n<br>\sum_{i=1}^{n}<br>x_i<br>]</p><h3>Variance</h3><h1>[<br>\sigma^2</h1><p>\frac1n<br>\sum_{i=1}^{n}<br>(x_i-\mu)^2<br>]</p><h3>Standard Deviation</h3><h1>[<br>\sigma</h1><p>\sqrt{\sigma^2}<br>]</p><h3>Probability</h3><h1>[<br>P(A)</h1><p>\frac{\text{Favorable Outcomes}}<br>{\text{Total Outcomes}}<br>]</p><h1>8. Digital Electronics</h1><p>Digital electronics relies on binary arithmetic and Boolean algebra.</p><h3>Binary to Decimal Conversion</h3><h1>[<br>N</h1><p>\sum_i<br>b_i2^i<br>]</p><h3>Boolean Identities</h3><p>[<br>A+A=A<br>]</p><p>[<br>A\cdot1=A<br>]</p><p>[<br>A+0=A<br>]</p><h1>9. Electrical Engineering</h1><p>Electrical engineering formulas describe relationships between voltage, current, resistance, and power.</p><h3>Ohm's Law</h3><h1>[<br>V</h1><p>IR<br>]</p><h3>Electric Power</h3><h1>[<br>P</h1><p>VI<br>]</p><h3>AC Power</h3><h1>[<br>P</h1><p>VI\cos\phi<br>]</p><h3>Electrical Energy</h3><h1>[<br>E</h1><p>Pt<br>]</p><h3>Efficiency</h3><h1>[<br>\eta</h1><p>\frac{\text{Output}}<br>{\text{Input}}<br>\times100%<br>]</p><h1>10. AC Machines</h1><p>AC motors are analyzed using speed and slip equations.</p><h3>Synchronous Speed</h3><h1>[<br>N_s</h1><p>\frac{120f}{P}<br>]</p><h3>Slip</h3><h1>[<br>s</h1><p>\frac{N_s-N_r}<br>{N_s}<br>]</p><h3>Rotor Frequency</h3><h1>[<br>f_r</h1><p>sf<br>]</p><h1>11. Physics</h1><p>Physics equations describe motion, force, and energy.</p><h3>Newton's Second Law</h3><h1>[<br>F</h1><p>ma<br>]</p><h3>Kinetic Energy</h3><h1>[<br>KE</h1><p>\frac12mv^2<br>]</p><h3>Potential Energy</h3><h1>[<br>PE</h1><p>mgh<br>]</p><h3>Momentum</h3><h1>[<br>p</h1><p>mv<br>]</p><h3>Density</h3><h1>[<br>\rho</h1><p>\frac{m}{V}<br>]</p><h1>12. Mathematics</h1><p>Mathematics provides the foundation for all scientific disciplines.</p><h3>Distance Formula</h3><h1>[<br>d</h1><p>\sqrt{(x_2-x_1)^2+(y_2-y_1)^2}<br>]</p><h3>Quadratic Formula</h3><h1>[<br>x</h1><p>\frac{-b\pm\sqrt{b^2-4ac}}<br>{2a}<br>]</p><h3>Area of a Circle</h3><h1>[<br>A</h1><p>\pi r^2<br>]</p><h3>Circumference of a Circle</h3><h1>[<br>C</h1><p>2\pi r<br>]</p><h3>Simple Interest</h3><h1>[<br>SI</h1><p>\frac{PRT}{100}<br>]</p><h3>Compound Interest</h3><h1>[<br>A</h1><p>P<br>\left(<br>1+\frac{r}{n}<br>\right)^{nt}<br>]</p><h3>Percentage</h3><h1>[<br>\text{Percentage}</h1><p>\frac{\text{Obtained Marks}}<br>{\text{Total Marks}}<br>\times100<br>]</p><h3>Average</h3><h1>[<br>\text{Average}</h1><p>\frac{\text{Sum of Values}}<br>{\text{Number of Values}}<br>]</p><h1>Conclusion</h1><p>Mathematical formulas are indispensable across engineering, computer science, artificial intelligence, networking, statistics, physics, and mathematics. They provide standardized methods for analyzing systems, optimizing algorithms, measuring performance, and solving complex problems. From evaluating CPU scheduling in operating systems and calculating network delays to training machine learning models and analyzing electrical circuits, these equations serve as the fundamental language of science and technology. A strong understanding of these formulas enables engineers, researchers, and students to design efficient systems, interpret experimental results, and develop innovative solutions across a wide range of technical disciplines.</p>

TRANSFORMED:

<!--StartFragment--><h1><strong>Engineering, Computer Science, and Mathematics Formula Handbook</strong></h1><h2>Introduction</h2><p>Mathematical formulas play a fundamental role in engineering, computer science, artificial intelligence, physics, and statistics. They provide precise methods for solving problems, analyzing systems, evaluating performance, and designing efficient algorithms. Whether calculating the execution time of a computer process, determining the efficiency of an electrical machine, training a neural network, or measuring statistical variation, mathematical equations form the backbone of scientific and technological advancements. This report presents a collection of commonly used formulas from various disciplines along with a brief explanation of their significance.</p><h1>1. Software Engineering and Operating Systems</h1><p>Software engineering and operating systems rely on performance metrics to evaluate process scheduling, CPU utilization, and system efficiency.</p><h3>Turnaround Time</h3><p>Turnaround time represents the total time taken for a process to complete execution after it arrives in the system.</p><p>[<br>\text{Turnaround Time} = \text{Completion Time} - \text{Arrival Time}<br>]</p><h3>Waiting Time</h3><p>Waiting time is the total duration a process spends waiting in the ready queue before execution.</p><p>[<br>\text{Waiting Time} = \text{Turnaround Time} - \text{Burst Time}<br>]</p><h3>Response Time</h3><p>Response time measures how quickly a process receives CPU attention after arriving.</p><p>[<br>\text{Response Time} = \text{First Response Time} - \text{Arrival Time}<br>]</p><h3>CPU Utilization</h3><p>CPU utilization indicates how efficiently the processor is being used.</p><h1>[<br>\text{CPU Utilization}</h1><p>\frac{\text{CPU Busy Time}}<br>{\text{Total Time}}<br>\times100%<br>]</p><h3>Throughput</h3><p>Throughput measures the number of processes completed in a unit of time.</p><h1>[<br>\text{Throughput}</h1><p>\frac{\text{Number of Processes}}<br>{\text{Total Time}}<br>]</p><h3>Effective Access Time</h3><p>This formula estimates the average memory access time considering page faults.</p><h1>[<br>EAT</h1><p>(1-p)m+pP<br>]</p><p>where:</p><ul><li><p>(m) = memory access time</p></li><li><p>(P) = page fault service time</p></li><li><p>(p) = page fault probability</p></li></ul><h3>Speedup</h3><p>Speedup compares the execution time before and after optimization.</p><h1>[<br>S</h1><p>\frac{\text{Old Execution Time}}<br>{\text{New Execution Time}}<br>]</p><h1>2. Computer Networks</h1><p>Computer networks use mathematical formulas to evaluate communication delays and transmission efficiency.</p><h3>Transmission Delay</h3><h1>[<br>T_t</h1><p>\frac{L}{R}<br>]</p><p>where:</p><ul><li><p>(L) = packet size</p></li><li><p>(R) = transmission rate</p></li></ul><h3>Propagation Delay</h3><h1>[<br>T_p</h1><p>\frac{d}{v}<br>]</p><p>where:</p><ul><li><p>(d) = transmission distance</p></li><li><p>(v) = propagation speed</p></li></ul><h3>Total Network Delay</h3><h1>[<br>D</h1><p>T_t<br>+<br>T_p<br>+<br>T_q<br>+<br>T_{proc}<br>]</p><p>where:</p><ul><li><p>(T_q) = queueing delay</p></li><li><p>(T_{proc}) = processing delay</p></li></ul><h3>Bandwidth Utilization</h3><h1>[<br>U</h1><p>\frac{\text{Useful Data}}<br>{\text{Total Bandwidth}}<br>]</p><h1>3. Database Systems</h1><p>Database systems use disk performance formulas to estimate data retrieval time.</p><h3>Disk Access Time</h3><h1>[<br>T</h1><p>T_s<br>+<br>T_r<br>+<br>T_t<br>]</p><p>where:</p><ul><li><p>(T_s) = seek time</p></li><li><p>(T_r) = rotational latency</p></li><li><p>(T_t) = transfer time</p></li></ul><h1>4. Software Testing</h1><p>Software testing metrics help evaluate software quality and testing effectiveness.</p><h3>Defect Density</h3><h1>[<br>\text{Defect Density}</h1><p>\frac{\text{Number of Defects}}<br>{\text{KLOC}}<br>]</p><h3>Test Coverage</h3><h1>[<br>\text{Coverage}</h1><p>\frac{\text{Executed Statements}}<br>{\text{Total Statements}}<br>\times100%<br>]</p><h3>Defect Removal Efficiency</h3><h1>[<br>DRE</h1><p>\frac{\text{Defects Removed}}<br>{\text{Total Defects}}<br>\times100%<br>]</p><h1>5. Compiler Design</h1><p>Compiler optimization often measures compression efficiency.</p><h3>Compression Ratio</h3><h1>[<br>CR</h1><p>\frac{\text{Original Size}}<br>{\text{Compressed Size}}<br>]</p><h1>6. Artificial Intelligence</h1><p>Artificial intelligence algorithms heavily depend on mathematical activation functions and optimization methods.</p><h3>Sigmoid Function</h3><h1>[<br>\sigma(x)</h1><p>\frac1{1+e^{-x}}<br>]</p><h3>ReLU Activation Function</h3><h1>[<br>f(x)</h1><p>\max(0,x)<br>]</p><h3>Softmax Function</h3><h1>[<br>P_i</h1><p>\frac{e^{x_i}}<br>{\sum_j e^{x_j}}<br>]</p><h3>Mean Squared Error</h3><h1>[<br>MSE</h1><p>\frac1n<br>\sum_{i=1}^{n}<br>(y_i-\hat y_i)^2<br>]</p><h3>Cross Entropy Loss</h3><h1>[<br>L</h1><p>-\sum_i<br>y_i<br>\log(\hat y_i)<br>]</p><h3>Gradient Descent</h3><h1>[<br>\theta</h1><h2>\theta</h2><p>\alpha<br>\nabla J(\theta)<br>]</p><h1>7. Statistics</h1><p>Statistics provides tools for analyzing and interpreting numerical data.</p><h3>Mean</h3><h1>[<br>\mu</h1><p>\frac1n<br>\sum_{i=1}^{n}<br>x_i<br>]</p><h3>Variance</h3><h1>[<br>\sigma^2</h1><p>\frac1n<br>\sum_{i=1}^{n}<br>(x_i-\mu)^2<br>]</p><h3>Standard Deviation</h3><h1>[<br>\sigma</h1><p>\sqrt{\sigma^2}<br>]</p><h3>Probability</h3><h1>[<br>P(A)</h1><p>\frac{\text{Favorable Outcomes}}<br>{\text{Total Outcomes}}<br>]</p><h1>8. Digital Electronics</h1><p>Digital electronics relies on binary arithmetic and Boolean algebra.</p><h3>Binary to Decimal Conversion</h3><h1>[<br>N</h1><p>\sum_i<br>b_i2^i<br>]</p><h3>Boolean Identities</h3><p>[<br>A+A=A<br>]</p><p>[<br>A\cdot1=A<br>]</p><p>[<br>A+0=A<br>]</p><h1>9. Electrical Engineering</h1><p>Electrical engineering formulas describe relationships between voltage, current, resistance, and power.</p><h3>Ohm's Law</h3><h1>[<br>V</h1><p>IR<br>]</p><h3>Electric Power</h3><h1>[<br>P</h1><p>VI<br>]</p><h3>AC Power</h3><h1>[<br>P</h1><p>VI\cos\phi<br>]</p><h3>Electrical Energy</h3><h1>[<br>E</h1><p>Pt<br>]</p><h3>Efficiency</h3><h1>[<br>\eta</h1><p>\frac{\text{Output}}<br>{\text{Input}}<br>\times100%<br>]</p><h1>10. AC Machines</h1><p>AC motors are analyzed using speed and slip equations.</p><h3>Synchronous Speed</h3><h1>[<br>N_s</h1><p>\frac{120f}{P}<br>]</p><h3>Slip</h3><h1>[<br>s</h1><p>\frac{N_s-N_r}<br>{N_s}<br>]</p><h3>Rotor Frequency</h3><h1>[<br>f_r</h1><p>sf<br>]</p><h1>11. Physics</h1><p>Physics equations describe motion, force, and energy.</p><h3>Newton's Second Law</h3><h1>[<br>F</h1><p>ma<br>]</p><h3>Kinetic Energy</h3><h1>[<br>KE</h1><p>\frac12mv^2<br>]</p><h3>Potential Energy</h3><h1>[<br>PE</h1><p>mgh<br>]</p><h3>Momentum</h3><h1>[<br>p</h1><p>mv<br>]</p><h3>Density</h3><h1>[<br>\rho</h1><p>\frac{m}{V}<br>]</p><h1>12. Mathematics</h1><p>Mathematics provides the foundation for all scientific disciplines.</p><h3>Distance Formula</h3><h1>[<br>d</h1><p>\sqrt{(x_2-x_1)^2+(y_2-y_1)^2}<br>]</p><h3>Quadratic Formula</h3><h1>[<br>x</h1><p>\frac{-b\pm\sqrt{b^2-4ac}}<br>{2a}<br>]</p><h3>Area of a Circle</h3><h1>[<br>A</h1><p>\pi r^2<br>]</p><h3>Circumference of a Circle</h3><h1>[<br>C</h1><p>2\pi r<br>]</p><h3>Simple Interest</h3><h1>[<br>SI</h1><p>\frac{PRT}{100}<br>]</p><h3>Compound Interest</h3><h1>[<br>A</h1><p>P<br>\left(<br>1+\frac{r}{n}<br>\right)^{nt}<br>]</p><h3>Percentage</h3><h1>[<br>\text{Percentage}</h1><p>\frac{\text{Obtained Marks}}<br>{\text{Total Marks}}<br>\times100<br>]</p><h3>Average</h3><h1>[<br>\text{Average}</h1><p>\frac{\text{Sum of Values}}<br>{\text{Number of Values}}<br>]</p><h1>Conclusion</h1><p>Mathematical formulas are indispensable across engineering, computer science, artificial intelligence, networking, statistics, physics, and mathematics. They provide standardized methods for analyzing systems, optimizing algorithms, measuring performance, and solving complex problems. From evaluating CPU scheduling in operating systems and calculating network delays to training machine learning models and analyzing electrical circuits, these equations serve as the fundamental language of science and technology. A strong understanding of these formulas enables engineers, researchers, and students to design efficient systems, interpret experimental results, and develop innovative solutions across a wide range of technical disciplines.</p>

Compiler Design</h1><p>Compiler optimization often measures compression efficiency.</p><h3>Compression Ratio</h3><h1>[<br>CR</h1><p>\frac{\text{Original Size}}<br>{\text{Compressed Size}}<br>]</p><h1>6. Artificial Intelligence</h1><p>Artificial intelligence algorithms heavily depend on mathematical activation functions and optimization methods.</p><h3>Sigmoid Function</h3><h1>[<br>\sigma(x)</h1><p>\frac1{1+e^{-x}}<br>]</p><h3>ReLU Activation Function</h3><h1>[<br>f(x)</h1><p>\max(0,x)<br>]</p><h3>Softmax Function</h3><h1>[<br>P_i</h1><p>\frac{e^{x_i}}<br>{\sum_j e^{x_j}}<br>]</p><h3>Mean Squared Error</h3><h1>[<br>MSE</h1><p>\frac1n<br>\sum_{i=1}^{n}<br>(y_i-\hat y_i)^2<br>]</p><h3>Cross Entropy Loss</h3><h1>[<br>L</h1><p>-\sum_i<br>y_i<br>\log(\hat y_i)<br>]</p><h3>Gradient Descent</h3><h1>[<br>\theta</h1><h2>\theta</h2><p>\alpha<br>\nabla J(\theta)<br>]</p><h1>7. Statistics</h1><p>Statistics provides tools for analyzing and interpreting numerical data.</p><h3>Mean</h3><h1>[<br>\mu</h1><p>\frac1n<br>\sum_{i=1}^{n}<br>x_i<br>]</p><h3>Variance</h3><h1>[<br>\sigma^2</h1><p>\frac1n<br>\sum_{i=1}^{n}<br>(x_i-\mu)^2<br>]</p><h3>Standard Deviation</h3><h1>[<br>\sigma</h1><p>\sqrt{\sigma^2}<br>]</p><h3>Probability</h3><h1>[<br>P(A)</h1><p>\frac{\text{Favorable Outcomes}}<br>{\text{Total Outcomes}}<br>]</p><h1>8. Digital Electronics</h1><p>Digital electronics relies on binary arithmetic and Boolean algebra.</p><h3>Binary to Decimal Conversion</h3><h1>[<br>N</h1><p>\sum_i<br>b_i2^i<br>]</p><h3>Boolean Identities</h3><p>[<br>A+A=A<br>]</p><p>[<br>A\cdot1=A<br>]</p><p>[<br>A+0=A<br>]</p><h1>9. Electrical Engineering</h1><p>Electrical engineering formulas describe relationships between voltage, current, resistance, and power.</p><h3>Ohm's Law</h3><h1>[<br>V</h1><p>IR<br>]</p><h3>Electric Power</h3><h1>[<br>P</h1><p>VI<br>]</p><h3>AC Power</h3><h1>[<br>P</h1><p>VI\cos\phi<br>]</p><h3>Electrical Energy</h3><h1>[<br>E</h1><p>Pt<br>]</p><h3>Efficiency</h3><h1>[<br>\eta</h1><p>\frac{\text{Output}}<br>{\text{Input}}<br>\times100%<br>]</p><h1>10. AC Machines</h1><p>AC motors are analyzed using speed and slip equations.</p><h3>Synchronous Speed</h3><h1>[<br>N_s</h1><p>\frac{120f}{P}<br>]</p><h3>Slip</h3><h1>[<br>s</h1><p>\frac{N_s-N_r}<br>{N_s}<br>]</p><h3>Rotor Frequency</h3><h1>[<br>f_r</h1><p>sf<br>]</p><h1>11. Physics</h1><p>Physics equations describe motion, force, and energy.</p><h3>Newton's Second Law</h3><h1>[<br>F</h1><p>ma<br>]</p><h3>Kinetic Energy</h3><h1>[<br>KE</h1><p>\frac12mv^2<br>]</p><h3>Potential Energy</h3><h1>[<br>PE</h1><p>mgh<br>]</p><h3>Momentum</h3><h1>[<br>p</h1><p>mv<br>]</p><h3>Density</h3><h1>[<br>\rho</h1><p>\frac{m}{V}<br>]</p><h1>12. Mathematics</h1><p>Mathematics provides the foundation for all scientific disciplines.</p><h3>Distance Formula</h3><h1>[<br>d</h1><p>\sqrt{(x_2-x_1)^2+(y_2-y_1)^2}<br>]</p><h3>Quadratic Formula</h3><h1>[<br>x</h1><p>\frac{-b\pm\sqrt{b^2-4ac}}<br>{2a}<br>]</p><h3>Area of a Circle</h3><h1>[<br>A</h1><p>\pi r^2<br>]</p><h3>Circumference of a Circle</h3><h1>[<br>C</h1><p>2\pi r<br>]</p><h3>Simple Interest</h3><h1>[<br>SI</h1><p>\frac{PRT}{100}<br>]</p><h3>Compound Interest</h3><h1>[<br>A</h1><p>P<br>\left(<br>1+\frac{r}{n}<br>\right)^{nt}<br>]</p><h3>Percentage</h3><h1>[<br>\text{Percentage}</h1><p>\frac{\text{Obtained Marks}}<br>{\text{Total Marks}}<br>\times100<br>]</p><h3>Average</h3><h1>[<br>\text{Average}</h1><p>\frac{\text{Sum of Values}}<br>{\text{Number of Values}}<br>]</p><h1>Conclusion</h1><p>Mathematical formulas are indispensable across engineering, computer science, artificial intelligence, networking, statistics, physics, and mathematics. They provide standardized methods for analyzing systems, optimizing algorithms, measuring performance, and solving complex problems. From evaluating CPU scheduling in operating systems and calculating network delays to training machine learning models and analyzing electrical circuits, these equations serve as the fundamental language of science and technology. A strong understanding of these formulas enables engineers, researchers, and students to design efficient systems, interpret experimental results, and develop innovative solutions across a wide range of technical disciplines.</p>

<h3 data-section-id="1f6tva9" data-start="6031" data-end="6052">Compound Interest</h3>
<span class="katex-display"><span class="katex"><span class="katex-mathml"><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="block"><semantics><mrow><mi>A</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>P</mi><msup><mrow><mo fence="true">(</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mi>r</mi><mi>n</mi></mfrac><mo fence="true">)</mo></mrow><mrow><mi>n</mi><mi>t</mi></mrow></msup></mrow><annotation encoding="application/x-tex">A
=
P
\left(
1+\frac{r}{n}
\right)^{nt}</annotation></semantics></math></span><span class="katex-html" aria-hidden="true"><span class="base"><span class="strut"></span><span class="mord mathnormal">A</span><span class="mspace"></span><span class="mrel">=</span><span class="mspace"></span></span><span class="base"><span class="strut"></span><span class="mord mathnormal">P</span><span class="mspace"></span><span class="minner"><span class="minner"><span class="mopen delimcenter"><span class="delimsizing size2">(</span></span><span class="mord">1</span><span class="mspace"></span><span class="mbin">+</span><span class="mspace"></span><span class="mord"><span class="mopen nulldelimiter"></span><span class="mfrac"><span class="vlist-t vlist-t2"><span class="vlist-r"><span class="vlist"><span><span class="pstrut"></span><span class="mord"><span class="mord mathnormal">n</span></span></span><span><span class="pstrut"></span><span class="frac-line"></span></span><span><span class="pstrut"></span><span class="mord"><span class="mord mathnormal">r</span></span></span></span><span class="vlist-s">​</span></span><span class="vlist-r"><span class="vlist"><span></span></span></span></span></span><span class="mclose nulldelimiter"></span></span><span class="mclose delimcenter"><span class="delimsizing size2">)</span></span></span><span class="msupsub"><span class="vlist-t"><span class="vlist-r"><span class="vlist"><span><span class="pstrut"></span><span class="sizing reset-size6 size3 mtight"><span class="mord mtight"><span class="mord mathnormal mtight">n</span><span class="mord mathnormal mtight">t</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span>
<h3 data-section-id="rbegji" data-start="6101" data-end="6115">Percentage</h3>
<span class="katex-display"><span class="katex"><span class="katex-mathml"><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="block"><semantics><mrow><mtext>Percentage</mtext><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mtext>Obtained&nbsp;Marks</mtext><mtext>Total&nbsp;Marks</mtext></mfrac><mo>×</mo><mn>100</mn></mrow><annotation encoding="application/x-tex">\text{Percentage}
=
\frac{\text{Obtained Marks}}
{\text{Total Marks}}
\times100</annotation></semantics></math></span><span class="katex-html" aria-hidden="true"><span class="base"><span class="strut"></span><span class="mord text"><span class="mord">Percentage</span></span><span class="mspace"></span><span class="mrel">=</span><span class="mspace"></span></span><span class="base"><span class="strut"></span><span class="mord"><span class="mopen nulldelimiter"></span><span class="mfrac"><span class="vlist-t vlist-t2"><span class="vlist-r"><span class="vlist"><span><span class="pstrut"></span><span class="mord"><span class="mord text"><span class="mord">Total&nbsp;Marks</span></span></span></span><span><span class="pstrut"></span><span class="frac-line"></span></span><span><span class="pstrut"></span><span class="mord"><span class="mord text"><span class="mord">Obtained&nbsp;Marks</span></span></span></span></span><span class="vlist-s">​</span></span><span class="vlist-r"><span class="vlist"><span></span></span></span></span></span><span class="mclose nulldelimiter"></span></span><span class="mspace"></span><span class="mbin">×</span><span class="mspace"></span></span><span class="base"><span class="strut"></span><span class="mord">100</span></span></span></span></span>
<h3 data-section-id="19w1kx1" data-start="6204" data-end="6215">Average</h3>
<span class="katex-display"><span class="katex"><span class="katex-mathml"><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="block"><semantics><mrow><mtext>Average</mtext><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mtext>Sum&nbsp;of&nbsp;Values</mtext><mtext>Number&nbsp;of&nbsp;Values</mtext></mfrac></mrow><annotation encoding="application/x-tex">\text{Average}
=
\frac{\text{Sum of Values}}
{\text{Number of Values}}</annotation></semantics></math></span><span class="katex-html" aria-hidden="true"><span class="base"><span class="strut"></span><span class="mord text"><span class="mord">Average</span></span><span class="mspace"></span><span class="mrel">=</span><span class="mspace"></span></span><span class="base"><span class="strut"></span><span class="mord"><span class="mopen nulldelimiter"></span><span class="mfrac"><span class="vlist-t vlist-t2"><span class="vlist-r"><span class="vlist"><span><span class="pstrut"></span><span class="mord"><span class="mord text"><span class="mord">Number&nbsp;of&nbsp;Values</span></span></span></span><span><span class="pstrut"></span><span class="frac-line"></span></span><span><span class="pstrut"></span><span class="mord"><span class="mord text"><span class="mord">Sum&nbsp;of&nbsp;Values</span></span></span></span></span><span class="vlist-s">​</span></span><span class="vlist-r"><span class="vlist"><span></span></span></span></span></span><span class="mclose nulldelimiter"></span></span></span></span></span></span>
<hr data-start="6295" data-end="6298">
<h1 data-section-id="fsb6xx" data-start="6300" data-end="6312">Conclusion</h1>
<p data-start="6314" data-end="7056" data-is-last-node="" data-is-only-node="" class="">Mathematical formulas are indispensable across engineering, computer science, artificial intelligence, networking, statistics, physics, and mathematics. They provide standardized methods for analyzing systems, optimizing algorithms, measuring performance, and solving complex problems. From evaluating CPU scheduling in operating systems and calculating network delays to training machine learning models and analyzing electrical circuits, these equations serve as the fundamental language of science and technology. A strong understanding of these formulas enables engineers, researchers, and students to design efficient systems, interpret experimental results, and develop innovative solutions across a wide range of technical disciplines.</p><!--EndFragment-->
</body>

<!--StartFragment--><h1 data-section-id="1c8rh7j" data-start="5674" data-end="5691" class="PDq2pG_selectionAnchorContainer">12. Mathematics<span aria-hidden="true" class="PDq2pG_selectionAnchor"></span></h1>
<p data-start="5693" data-end="5760">Mathematics provides the foundation for all scientific disciplines.</p>
<h3 data-section-id="y3kkkr" data-start="5762" data-end="5782">Distance Formula</h3>
<span data-latex="d
=
\sqrt{(x_2-x_1)^2+(y_2-y_1)^2}"></span>
<h3 data-section-id="1usjbd0" data-start="5826" data-end="5847">Quadratic Formula</h3>
<span data-latex="x
=
\frac{-b\pm\sqrt{b^2-4ac}}
{2a}"></span>
<h3 data-section-id="f6qf9n" data-start="5892" data-end="5912">Area of a Circle</h3>
<span data-latex="A
=
\pi r^2"></span>
<h3 data-section-id="1k69sv7" data-start="5933" data-end="5962" class="">Circumference of a Circle</h3>
<span data-latex="C
=
2\pi r"></span>
<h3 data-section-id="mfnsz2" data-start="5982" data-end="6001">Simple Interest</h3>
<span data-latex="SI
=
\frac{PRT}{100}"></span>
<h3 data-section-id="1f6tva9" data-start="6031" data-end="6052">Compound Interest</h3>
<span data-latex="A
=
P
\left(
1+\frac{r}{n}
\right)^{nt}"></span>
<h3 data-section-id="rbegji" data-start="6101" data-end="6115">Percentage</h3>
<span data-latex="\text{Percentage}
=
\frac{\text{Obtained Marks}}
{\text{Total Marks}}
\times100"></span>
<h3 data-section-id="19w1kx1" data-start="6204" data-end="6215">Average</h3>
<span data-latex="\text{Average}
=
\frac{\text{Sum of Values}}
{\text{Number of Values}}"></span>

<h1 data-section-id="fsb6xx" data-start="6300" data-end="6312">Conclusion</h1>
<p data-start="6314" data-end="7056" data-is-last-node="" data-is-only-node="" class="">Mathematical formulas are indispensable across engineering, computer science, artificial intelligence, networking, statistics, physics, and mathematics. They provide standardized methods for analyzing systems, optimizing algorithms, measuring performance, and solving complex problems. From evaluating CPU scheduling in operating systems and calculating network delays to training machine learning models and analyzing electrical circuits, these equations serve as the fundamental language of science and technology. A strong understanding of these formulas enables engineers, researchers, and students to design efficient systems, interpret experimental results, and develop innovative solutions across a wide range of technical disciplines.</p><!--EndFragment-->

<h1 data-section-id="fsb6xx" data-start="6300" data-end="6312">Conclusion</h1>
<p data-start="6314" data-end="7056" data-is-last-node="" data-is-only-node="" class="">Mathematical formulas are indispensable across engineering, computer science, artificial intelligence, networking, statistics, physics, and mathematics. They provide standardized methods for analyzing systems, optimizing algorithms, measuring performance, and solving complex problems. From evaluating CPU scheduling in operating systems and calculating network delays to training machine learning models and analyzing electrical circuits, these equations serve as the fundamental language of science and technology. A strong understanding of these formulas enables engineers, researchers, and students to design efficient systems, interpret experimental results, and develop innovative solutions across a wide range of technical disciplines.</p><!--EndFragment-->

<!--StartFragment--><html><head></head><body><h1><strong>Engineering, Computer Science, and Mathematics Formula Handbook</strong></h1><h2>Introduction</h2><p>Mathematical formulas play a fundamental role in engineering, computer science, artificial intelligence, physics, and statistics. They provide precise methods for solving problems, analyzing systems, evaluating performance, and designing efficient algorithms. Whether calculating the execution time of a computer process, determining the efficiency of an electrical machine, training a neural network, or measuring statistical variation, mathematical equations form the backbone of scientific and technological advancements. This report presents a collection of commonly used formulas from various disciplines along with a brief explanation of their significance.</p><hr><h1>1. Software Engineering and Operating Systems</h1><p>Software engineering and operating systems rely on performance metrics to evaluate process scheduling, CPU utilization, and system efficiency.</p><h3>Turnaround Time</h3><p>Turnaround time represents the total time taken for a process to complete execution after it arrives in the system.</p><p>[<br>\text{Turnaround Time} = \text{Completion Time} - \text{Arrival Time}<br>]</p><h3>Waiting Time</h3><p>Waiting time is the total duration a process spends waiting in the ready queue before execution.</p><p>[<br>\text{Waiting Time} = \text{Turnaround Time} - \text{Burst Time}<br>]</p><h3>Response Time</h3><p>Response time measures how quickly a process receives CPU attention after arriving.</p><p>[<br>\text{Response Time} = \text{First Response Time} - \text{Arrival Time}<br>]</p><h3>CPU Utilization</h3><p>CPU utilization indicates how efficiently the processor is being used.</p><h1>[<br>\text{CPU Utilization}</h1><p>\frac{\text{CPU Busy Time}}<br>{\text{Total Time}}<br>\times100%<br>]</p><h3>Throughput</h3><p>Throughput measures the number of processes completed in a unit of time.</p><h1>[<br>\text{Throughput}</h1><p>\frac{\text{Number of Processes}}<br>{\text{Total Time}}<br>]</p><h3>Effective Access Time</h3><p>This formula estimates the average memory access time considering page faults.</p><h1>[<br>EAT</h1><p>(1-p)m+pP<br>]</p><p>where:</p><ul><li><p>(m) = memory access time</p></li><li><p>(P) = page fault service time</p></li><li><p>(p) = page fault probability</p></li></ul><h3>Speedup</h3><p>Speedup compares the execution time before and after optimization.</p><h1>[<br>S</h1><p>\frac{\text{Old Execution Time}}<br>{\text{New Execution Time}}<br>]</p><hr><h1>2. Computer Networks</h1><p>Computer networks use mathematical formulas to evaluate communication delays and transmission efficiency.</p><h3>Transmission Delay</h3><h1>[<br>T_t</h1><p>\frac{L}{R}<br>]</p><p>where:</p><ul><li><p>(L) = packet size</p></li><li><p>(R) = transmission rate</p></li></ul><h3>Propagation Delay</h3><h1>[<br>T_p</h1><p>\frac{d}{v}<br>]</p><p>where:</p><ul><li><p>(d) = transmission distance</p></li><li><p>(v) = propagation speed</p></li></ul><h3>Total Network Delay</h3><h1>[<br>D</h1><p>T_t<br>+<br>T_p<br>+<br>T_q<br>+<br>T_{proc}<br>]</p><p>where:</p><ul><li><p>(T_q) = queueing delay</p></li><li><p>(T_{proc}) = processing delay</p></li></ul><h3>Bandwidth Utilization</h3><h1>[<br>U</h1><p>\frac{\text{Useful Data}}<br>{\text{Total Bandwidth}}<br>]</p><hr><h1>3. Database Systems</h1><p>Database systems use disk performance formulas to estimate data retrieval time.</p><h3>Disk Access Time</h3><h1>[<br>T</h1><p>T_s<br>+<br>T_r<br>+<br>T_t<br>]</p><p>where:</p><ul><li><p>(T_s) = seek time</p></li><li><p>(T_r) = rotational latency</p></li><li><p>(T_t) = transfer time</p></li></ul><hr><h1>4. Software Testing</h1><p>Software testing metrics help evaluate software quality and testing effectiveness.</p><h3>Defect Density</h3><h1>[<br>\text{Defect Density}</h1><p>\frac{\text{Number of Defects}}<br>{\text{KLOC}}<br>]</p><h3>Test Coverage</h3><h1>[<br>\text{Coverage}</h1><p>\frac{\text{Executed Statements}}<br>{\text{Total Statements}}<br>\times100%<br>]</p><h3>Defect Removal Efficiency</h3><h1>[<br>DRE</h1><p>\frac{\text{Defects Removed}}<br>{\text{Total Defects}}<br>\times100%<br>]</p><hr><h1>5. Compiler Design</h1><p>Compiler optimization often measures compression efficiency.</p><h3>Compression Ratio</h3><h1>[<br>CR</h1><p>\frac{\text{Original Size}}<br>{\text{Compressed Size}}<br>]</p><hr><h1>6. Artificial Intelligence</h1><p>Artificial intelligence algorithms heavily depend on mathematical activation functions and optimization methods.</p><h3>Sigmoid Function</h3><h1>[<br>\sigma(x)</h1><p>\frac1{1+e^{-x}}<br>]</p><h3>ReLU Activation Function</h3><h1>[<br>f(x)</h1><p>\max(0,x)<br>]</p><h3>Softmax Function</h3><h1>[<br>P_i</h1><p>\frac{e^{x_i}}<br>{\sum_j e^{x_j}}<br>]</p><h3>Mean Squared Error</h3><h1>[<br>MSE</h1><p>\frac1n<br>\sum_{i=1}^{n}<br>(y_i-\hat y_i)^2<br>]</p><h3>Cross Entropy Loss</h3><h1>[<br>L</h1><p>-\sum_i<br>y_i<br>\log(\hat y_i)<br>]</p><h3>Gradient Descent</h3><h1>[<br>\theta</h1><h2>\theta</h2><p>\alpha<br>\nabla J(\theta)<br>]</p><hr><h1>7. Statistics</h1><p>Statistics provides tools for analyzing and interpreting numerical data.</p><h3>Mean</h3><h1>[<br>\mu</h1><p>\frac1n<br>\sum_{i=1}^{n}<br>x_i<br>]</p><h3>Variance</h3><h1>[<br>\sigma^2</h1><p>\frac1n<br>\sum_{i=1}^{n}<br>(x_i-\mu)^2<br>]</p><h3>Standard Deviation</h3><h1>[<br>\sigma</h1><p>\sqrt{\sigma^2}<br>]</p><h3>Probability</h3><h1>[<br>P(A)</h1><p>\frac{\text{Favorable Outcomes}}<br>{\text{Total Outcomes}}<br>]</p><hr><h1>8. Digital Electronics</h1><p>Digital electronics relies on binary arithmetic and Boolean algebra.</p><h3>Binary to Decimal Conversion</h3><h1>[<br>N</h1><p>\sum_i<br>b_i2^i<br>]</p><h3>Boolean Identities</h3><p>[<br>A+A=A<br>]</p><p>[<br>A\cdot1=A<br>]</p><p>[<br>A+0=A<br>]</p><hr><h1>9. Electrical Engineering</h1><p>Electrical engineering formulas describe relationships between voltage, current, resistance, and power.</p><h3>Ohm's Law</h3><h1>[<br>V</h1><p>IR<br>]</p><h3>Electric Power</h3><h1>[<br>P</h1><p>VI<br>]</p><h3>AC Power</h3><h1>[<br>P</h1><p>VI\cos\phi<br>]</p><h3>Electrical Energy</h3><h1>[<br>E</h1><p>Pt<br>]</p><h3>Efficiency</h3><h1>[<br>\eta</h1><p>\frac{\text{Output}}<br>{\text{Input}}<br>\times100%<br>]</p><hr><h1>10. AC Machines</h1><p>AC motors are analyzed using speed and slip equations.</p><h3>Synchronous Speed</h3><h1>[<br>N_s</h1><p>\frac{120f}{P}<br>]</p><h3>Slip</h3><h1>[<br>s</h1><p>\frac{N_s-N_r}<br>{N_s}<br>]</p><h3>Rotor Frequency</h3><h1>[<br>f_r</h1><p>sf<br>]</p><hr><h1>11. Physics</h1><p>Physics equations describe motion, force, and energy.</p><h3>Newton's Second Law</h3><h1>[<br>F</h1><p>ma<br>]</p><h3>Kinetic Energy</h3><h1>[<br>KE</h1><p>\frac12mv^2<br>]</p><h3>Potential Energy</h3><h1>[<br>PE</h1><p>mgh<br>]</p><h3>Momentum</h3><h1>[<br>p</h1><p>mv<br>]</p><h3>Density</h3><h1>[<br>\rho</h1><p>\frac{m}{V}<br>]</p><hr><h1>12. Mathematics</h1><p>Mathematics provides the foundation for all scientific disciplines.</p><h3>Distance Formula</h3><h1>[<br>d</h1><p>\sqrt{(x_2-x_1)^2+(y_2-y_1)^2}<br>]</p><h3>Quadratic Formula</h3><h1>[<br>x</h1><p>\frac{-b\pm\sqrt{b^2-4ac}}<br>{2a}<br>]</p><h3>Area of a Circle</h3><h1>[<br>A</h1><p>\pi r^2<br>]</p><h3>Circumference of a Circle</h3><h1>[<br>C</h1><p>2\pi r<br>]</p><h3>Simple Interest</h3><h1>[<br>SI</h1><p>\frac{PRT}{100}<br>]</p><h3>Compound Interest</h3><h1>[<br>A</h1><p>P<br>\left(<br>1+\frac{r}{n}<br>\right)^{nt}<br>]</p><h3>Percentage</h3><h1>[<br>\text{Percentage}</h1><p>\frac{\text{Obtained Marks}}<br>{\text{Total Marks}}<br>\times100<br>]</p><h3>Average</h3><h1>[<br>\text{Average}</h1><p>\frac{\text{Sum of Values}}<br>{\text{Number of Values}}<br>]</p><hr><h1>Conclusion</h1><p>Mathematical formulas are indispensable across engineering, computer science, artificial intelligence, networking, statistics, physics, and mathematics. They provide standardized methods for analyzing systems, optimizing algorithms, measuring performance, and solving complex problems. From evaluating CPU scheduling in operating systems and calculating network delays to training machine learning models and analyzing electrical circuits, these equations serve as the fundamental language of science and technology. A strong understanding of these formulas enables engineers, researchers, and students to design efficient systems, interpret experimental results, and develop innovative solutions across a wide range of technical disciplines.</p></body></html><!--EndFragment-->
</body>
</html>
