{\text{Number of Values}}"></span>

<h1 data-section-id="fsb6xx" data-start="6300" data-end="6312">Conclusion</h1>
<p data-start="6314" data-end="7056" data-is-last-node="" data-is-only-node="" class="">Mathematical formulas are indispensable across engineering, computer science, artificial intelligence, networking, statistics, physics, and mathematics. They provide standardized methods for analyzing systems, optimizing algorithms, measuring performance, and solving complex problems. From evaluating CPU scheduling in operating systems and calculating network delays to training machine learning models and analyzing electrical circuits, these equations serve as the fundamental language of science and technology. A strong understanding of these formulas enables engineers, researchers, and students to design efficient systems, interpret experimental results, and develop innovative solutions across a wide range of technical disciplines.</p><!--EndFragment-->

ORIGINAL:
<html>
<body>
<!--StartFragment--><html><head></head><body><h1>Transformer Architecture: A Short Report</h1><h2>Introduction</h2><p>The Transformer is a deep learning architecture introduced in the paper <em>Attention Is All You Need</em> (2017). Unlike recurrent neural networks (RNNs), Transformers process all input tokens simultaneously using a mechanism called <strong>self-attention</strong>, enabling efficient parallel computation and improved performance on long sequences. Today, Transformers form the foundation of modern large language models such as GPT, BERT, and many multimodal AI systems.</p><h2>Architecture Overview</h2><p>A standard Transformer consists of two major components:</p><ul><li><p><strong>Encoder</strong>: Converts the input sequence into contextual representations.</p></li><li><p><strong>Decoder</strong>: Generates the output sequence using encoder representations and previously generated tokens.</p></li></ul><p>Many modern models use only one of these components:</p><ul><li><p>Encoder-only (e.g., BERT)</p></li><li><p>Decoder-only (e.g., GPT)</p></li><li><p>Encoder-decoder (e.g., T5)</p></li></ul><h2>Self-Attention</h2><p>For an input matrix</p><p>[<br>X \in \mathbb{R}^{n \times d}<br>]</p><p>three matrices are computed:</p><p>[<br>Q = XW_Q<br>]</p><p>[<br>K = XW_K<br>]</p><p>[<br>V = XW_V<br>]</p><p>where:</p><ul><li><p>(Q) = Query matrix</p></li><li><p>(K) = Key matrix</p></li><li><p>(V) = Value matrix</p></li></ul><p>The scaled dot-product attention is</p><h1>[<br>\mathrm{Attention}(Q,K,V)</h1><p>\mathrm{softmax}<br>\left(<br>\frac{QK^{T}}{\sqrt{d_k}}<br>\right)V<br>]</p><p>where (d_k) is the key dimension.</p><h2>Multi-Head Attention</h2><p>Instead of using a single attention operation, multiple attention heads learn different relationships simultaneously.</p><p>For each head:</p><h1>[<br>\text{head}_i</h1><p>\mathrm{Attention}<br>(Q_i,K_i,V_i)<br>]</p><p>The outputs are concatenated:</p><h1>[<br>\mathrm{MultiHead}(Q,K,V)</h1><p>\mathrm{Concat}<br>(\text{head}_1,\ldots,\text{head}_h)<br>W_O<br>]</p><p>where (h) is the number of attention heads.</p><h2>Position-wise Feed Forward Network</h2><p>Each token is independently passed through a feed-forward neural network:</p><h1>[<br>\mathrm{FFN}(x)</h1><p>\max(0,xW_1+b_1)W_2+b_2<br>]</p><p>The ReLU activation is often replaced with GELU in modern Transformer variants.</p><h2>Positional Encoding</h2><p>Since Transformers process all tokens simultaneously, positional information is added using sinusoidal encoding:</p><h1>[<br>PE(pos,2i)</h1><p>\sin<br>\left(<br>\frac{pos}{10000^{2i/d}}<br>\right)<br>]</p><h1>[<br>PE(pos,2i+1)</h1><p>\cos<br>\left(<br>\frac{pos}{10000^{2i/d}}<br>\right)<br>]</p><p>where:</p><ul><li><p>(pos) = token position</p></li><li><p>(i) = embedding dimension index</p></li></ul><h2>Residual Connections and Layer Normalization</h2><p>Each sub-layer uses a residual connection followed by layer normalization:</p><h1>[<br>\mathrm{Output}</h1><p>\mathrm{LayerNorm}<br>(x+\mathrm{Sublayer}(x))<br>]</p><p>This improves gradient flow and stabilizes training.</p><h2>Advantages</h2><ul><li><p>Parallel processing of sequences</p></li><li><p>Captures long-range dependencies effectively</p></li><li><p>Scales efficiently to large datasets</p></li><li><p>State-of-the-art performance across NLP, vision, speech, and multimodal tasks</p></li></ul><h2>Applications</h2><p>Transformers are widely used in:</p><ul><li><p>Large Language Models (GPT, Llama, Claude)</p></li><li><p>Machine Translation</p></li><li><p>Text Summarization</p></li><li><p>Question Answering</p></li><li><p>Image Classification (Vision Transformers)</p></li><li><p>Speech Recognition</p></li><li><p>Multimodal AI</p></li></ul><h2>Conclusion</h2><p>The Transformer architecture revolutionized deep learning by replacing recurrence with self-attention. Its ability to model relationships between all tokens simultaneously enables efficient training, superior scalability, and outstanding performance across a wide range of artificial intelligence tasks. The self-attention mechanism, combined with multi-head attention, positional encoding, residual connections, and feed-forward networks, remains the foundation of most modern AI systems.</p></body></html><!--EndFragment-->
</body>
</html>

TRANSFORMED:

<!--StartFragment--><h1>Transformer Architecture: A Short Report</h1><h2>Introduction</h2><p>The Transformer is a deep learning architecture introduced in the paper <em>Attention Is All You Need</em> (2017). Unlike recurrent neural networks (RNNs), Transformers process all input tokens simultaneously using a mechanism called <strong>self-attention</strong>, enabling efficient parallel computation and improved performance on long sequences. Today, Transformers form the foundation of modern large language models such as GPT, BERT, and many multimodal AI systems.</p><h2>Architecture Overview</h2><p>A standard Transformer consists of two major components:</p><ul><li><p><strong>Encoder</strong>: Converts the input sequence into contextual representations.</p></li><li><p><strong>Decoder</strong>: Generates the output sequence using encoder representations and previously generated tokens.</p></li></ul><p>Many modern models use only one of these components:</p><ul><li><p>Encoder-only (e.g., BERT)</p></li><li><p>Decoder-only (e.g., GPT)</p></li><li><p>Encoder-decoder (e.g., T5)</p></li></ul><h2>Self-Attention</h2><p>For an input matrix</p><p>[<br>X \in \mathbb{R}^{n \times d}<br>]</p><p>three matrices are computed:</p><p>[<br>Q = XW_Q<br>]</p><p>[<br>K = XW_K<br>]</p><p>[<br>V = XW_V<br>]</p><p>where:</p><ul><li><p>(Q) = Query matrix</p></li><li><p>(K) = Key matrix</p></li><li><p>(V) = Value matrix</p></li></ul><p>The scaled dot-product attention is</p><h1>[<br>\mathrm{Attention}(Q,K,V)</h1><p>\mathrm{softmax}<br>\left(<br>\frac{QK^{T}}{\sqrt{d_k}}<br>\right)V<br>]</p><p>where (d_k) is the key dimension.</p><h2>Multi-Head Attention</h2><p>Instead of using a single attention operation, multiple attention heads learn different relationships simultaneously.</p><p>For each head:</p><h1>[<br>\text{head}_i</h1><p>\mathrm{Attention}<br>(Q_i,K_i,V_i)<br>]</p><p>The outputs are concatenated:</p><h1>[<br>\mathrm{MultiHead}(Q,K,V)</h1><p>\mathrm{Concat}<br>(\text{head}_1,\ldots,\text{head}_h)<br>W_O<br>]</p><p>where (h) is the number of attention heads.</p><h2>Position-wise Feed Forward Network</h2><p>Each token is independently passed through a feed-forward neural network:</p><h1>[<br>\mathrm{FFN}(x)</h1><p>\max(0,xW_1+b_1)W_2+b_2<br>]</p><p>The ReLU activation is often replaced with GELU in modern Transformer variants.</p><h2>Positional Encoding</h2><p>Since Transformers process all tokens simultaneously, positional information is added using sinusoidal encoding:</p><h1>[<br>PE(pos,2i)</h1><p>\sin<br>\left(<br>\frac{pos}{10000^{2i/d}}<br>\right)<br>]</p><h1>[<br>PE(pos,2i+1)</h1><p>\cos<br>\left(<br>\frac{pos}{10000^{2i/d}}<br>\right)<br>]</p><p>where:</p><ul><li><p>(pos) = token position</p></li><li><p>(i) = embedding dimension index</p></li></ul><h2>Residual Connections and Layer Normalization</h2><p>Each sub-layer uses a residual connection followed by layer normalization:</p><h1>[<br>\mathrm{Output}</h1><p>\mathrm{LayerNorm}<br>(x+\mathrm{Sublayer}(x))<br>]</p><p>This improves gradient flow and stabilizes training.</p><h2>Advantages</h2><ul><li><p>Parallel processing of sequences</p></li><li><p>Captures long-range dependencies effectively</p></li><li><p>Scales efficiently to large datasets</p></li><li><p>State-of-the-art performance across NLP, vision, speech, and multimodal tasks</p></li></ul><h2>Applications</h2><p>Transformers are widely used in:</p><ul><li><p>Large Language Models (GPT, Llama, Claude)</p></li><li><p>Machine Translation</p></li><li><p>Text Summarization</p></li><li><p>Question Answering</p></li><li><p>Image Classification (Vision Transformers)</p></li><li><p>Speech Recognition</p></li><li><p>Multimodal AI</p></li></ul><h2>Conclusion</h2><p>The Transformer architecture revolutionized deep learning by replacing recurrence with self-attention. Its ability to model relationships between all tokens simultaneously enables efficient training, superior scalability, and outstanding performance across a wide range of artificial intelligence tasks. The self-attention mechanism, combined with multi-head attention, positional encoding, residual connections, and feed-forward networks, remains the foundation of most modern AI systems.</p>

<h1 data-section-id="1e4gaa1" data-start="1046" data-end="1068">3. Linear Regression</h1>
<p data-start="1070" data-end="1078">Contains</p>

<p data-start="3392" data-end="3399">Grammar</p>
<pre class="overflow-visible! px-0!" data-start="3401" data-end="3459"><div class="relative w-full mt-4 mb-1"><div class=""><div class="contents"><div class="relative"><div class="h-full min-h-0 min-w-0"><div class="h-full min-h-0 min-w-0"><div class="border border-token-border-light border-radius-3xl corner-superellipse/1.1 rounded-3xl"><div class="h-full w-full border-radius-3xl bg-(--code-block-surface) corner-superellipse/1.1 overflow-clip rounded-3xl [--code-block-surface:var(--bg-elevated-secondary)] dark:[--code-block-surface:var(--composer-surface-primary)] lxnfua_clipPathFallback"><div class="pointer-events-none absolute end-1.5 top-1 z-2 md:end-2 md:top-1"></div><div class="relative"><div class="pe-11 pt-3"><div class="relative z-0 flex max-w-full"><div id="code-block-viewer" dir="ltr" class="q9tKkq_viewer cm-editor z-10 light:cm-light dark:cm-light flex h-full w-full flex-col items-stretch ͼs ͼ16"><div class="cm-scroller"><pre class="cm-content q9tKkq_readonly m-0"><code><span>E → E + T

F → id</span></code></pre></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div><div class=""><div class=""></div></div></div></div></div></div></pre>
<hr data-start="3461" data-end="3464">
<h1 data-section-id="mkv0x6" data-start="3466" data-end="3498">16. IEEE Research Paper Sample</h1>
<p data-start="3500" data-end="3612">This is probably the <strong data-start="3521" data-end="3549">best overall stress test</strong> because it naturally combines almost every formatting feature.</p>
<p data-start="3614" data-end="3626">It includes:</p>
<ul data-start="3628" data-end="3937">
<li data-section-id="16mauaw" data-start="3628" data-end="3638">
Abstract
</li>
<li data-section-id="ptaobm" data-start="3639" data-end="3649">
Keywords
</li>
<li data-section-id="1pq9wb9" data-start="3650" data-end="3675">
Multiple heading levels
</li>
<li data-section-id="qqhx0h" data-start="3676" data-end="3704">
<strong data-start="3678" data-end="3686">Bold</strong> and <em data-start="3691" data-end="3699">italic</em> text
</li>
<li data-section-id="1jzwefi" data-start="3705" data-end="3723">
Inline equations
</li>
<li data-section-id="1ops8v1" data-start="3724" data-end="3743">
Display equations
</li>
<li data-section-id="1dgkhzz" data-start="3744" data-end="3758">
Bullet lists
</li>
<li data-section-id="135cfff" data-start="3759" data-end="3775">
Numbered lists
</li>
<li data-section-id="5s09k5" data-start="3776" data-end="3784">
Tables
</li>
<li data-section-id="10l6ezy" data-start="3785" data-end="3808">
Figures with captions
</li>
<li data-section-id="c7xda7" data-start="3809" data-end="3822">
Code blocks
</li>
<li data-section-id="15vkgf0" data-start="3823" data-end="3856">
Citations like <strong data-start="3840" data-end="3847">[1]</strong>, <strong data-start="3849" data-end="3856">[2]</strong>
</li>
<li data-section-id="ripcs0" data-start="3857" data-end="3869">
References
</li>
<li data-section-id="1244rfd" data-start="3870" data-end="3881">
Footnotes
</li>
<li data-section-id="1n1bgaz" data-start="3882" data-end="3937">
Mixed paragraphs containing both text and mathematics
</li>
</ul>
<hr data-start="3939" data-end="3942">
<h2 data-section-id="1tzun89" data-start="3944" data-end="3974">Ultimate Stress-Test Report</h2>
<p data-start="3976" data-end="4138">If your goal is to build something like FormatMagic, create a single <strong data-start="4045" data-end="4069">15 to 20 page report</strong> titled <strong data-start="4077" data-end="4115">"Modern Computer Science Handbook"</strong> with chapters such as:</p>
<ol data-start="4140" data-end="4437">
<li data-section-id="1vrv4fl" data-start="4140" data-end="4160">
Operating Systems
</li>
<li data-section-id="zy8j3n" data-start="4161" data-end="4181">
Computer Networks
</li>
<li data-section-id="1tk5o8b" data-start="4182" data-end="4201">
Database Systems
</li>
<li data-section-id="quwaau" data-start="4202" data-end="4221">
Software Testing
</li>
<li data-section-id="1vvm1an" data-start="4222" data-end="4240">
Compiler Design
</li>
<li data-section-id="yr0fji" data-start="4241" data-end="4267">
Artificial Intelligence
</li>
<li data-section-id="1wjdmp9" data-start="4268" data-end="4287">
Machine Learning
</li>
<li data-section-id="1bcxkt9" data-start="4288" data-end="4315">
Transformer Architecture
</li>
<li data-section-id="1amosqf" data-start="4316" data-end="4329">
Statistics
</li>
<li data-section-id="mgic2p" data-start="4330" data-end="4341">
Physics
</li>
<li data-section-id="1kwu8l8" data-start="4342" data-end="4368">
Electrical Engineering
</li>
<li data-section-id="7z29dm" data-start="4369" data-end="4381">
Calculus
</li>
<li data-section-id="1x4d9qa" data-start="4382" data-end="4405">
Digital Electronics
</li>
<li data-section-id="fg3i6w" data-start="4406" data-end="4422">
Cryptography
</li>
<li data-section-id="magjef" data-start="4423" data-end="4437">
References
</li>
</ol>
<p data-start="4439" data-end="4779" data-is-last-node="" data-is-only-node="">This one document will exercise virtually every parser in your pipeline: headings, nested lists, inline and display math, tables, code fences, citations, references, bold/italic text, and mixed-content paragraphs. If your formatter can preserve that cleanly from copy-paste, it's a far more convincing benchmark than a dozen simple reports.</p><!--EndFragment-->
</body>

<h2 data-section-id="1tzun89" data-start="3944" data-end="3974">Ultimate Stress-Test Report</h2>
<p data-start="3976" data-end="4138">If your goal is to build something like FormatMagic, create a single <strong data-start="4045" data-end="4069">15 to 20 page report</strong> titled <strong data-start="4077" data-end="4115">"Modern Computer Science Handbook"</strong> with chapters such as:</p>
<ol data-start="4140" data-end="4437">
<li data-section-id="1vrv4fl" data-start="4140" data-end="4160">
Operating Systems
</li>
<li data-section-id="zy8j3n" data-start="4161" data-end="4181">
Computer Networks
</li>
<li data-section-id="1tk5o8b" data-start="4182" data-end="4201">
Database Systems
</li>
<li data-section-id="quwaau" data-start="4202" data-end="4221">
Software Testing
</li>
<li data-section-id="1vvm1an" data-start="4222" data-end="4240">

<p data-start="2117" data-end="2126">Equations</p>
<span class="katex-display"><span class="katex"><span class="katex-mathml"><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="block"><semantics><mrow><mfrac><mi>d</mi><mrow><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfrac><msup><mi>x</mi><mi>n</mi></msup><mo>=</mo><mi>n</mi><msup><mi>x</mi><mrow><mi>n</mi><mo>−</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></mrow><annotation encoding="application/x-tex">\frac{d}{dx}x^n
=

<span class="katex-display"><span class="katex"><span class="katex-mathml"><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="block"><semantics><mrow><mo>∫</mo><msup><mi>x</mi><mi>n</mi></msup><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mfrac><msup><mi>x</mi><mrow><mi>n</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mrow><mi>n</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfrac><mo>+</mo><mi>C</mi></mrow><annotation encoding="application/x-tex">\int x^n dx

<p data-start="2877" data-end="2889">Example code</p>
<pre class="overflow-visible! px-0!" data-start="2891" data-end="2978"><div class="relative w-full mt-4 mb-1"><div class=""><div class="contents"><div class="border border-token-border-light border-radius-3xl corner-superellipse/1.1 rounded-3xl"><div class="relative h-full w-full border-radius-3xl bg-(--code-block-surface) corner-superellipse/1.1 overflow-clip rounded-3xl [--code-block-surface:var(--bg-elevated-secondary)] dark:[--code-block-surface:var(--composer-surface-primary)] lxnfua_clipPathFallback"><div class="pointer-events-none absolute inset-x-4 top-12 bottom-4"><div class="pointer-events-none sticky z-40 shrink-0 z-1!"><div class="sticky bg-token-border-light"></div></div></div><div class="relative"><div class="h-full min-h-0 min-w-0"><div class="h-full min-h-0 min-w-0"><div class=""><div class="relative"><div class=""><div class="relative z-0 flex max-w-full"><div id="code-block-viewer" dir="ltr" class="q9tKkq_viewer cm-editor z-10 light:cm-light dark:cm-light flex h-full w-full flex-col items-stretch ͼs ͼ16"><div class="cm-scroller"><pre class="cm-content q9tKkq_readonly m-0"><code><span class="ͼv">SELECT</span><span> name, salary
</span><span class="ͼv">FROM</span><span> Employee
</span><span class="ͼv">WHERE</span><span> salary </span><span class="ͼv">&gt;</span><span> </span><span class="ͼy">50000</span><span>
</span><span class="ͼv">ORDER</span><span> </span><span class="ͼv">BY</span><span> salary </span><span class="ͼv">DESC</span><span>;</span></code></pre></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div><div class=""><div class=""></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></pre>
<p data-start="2980" data-end="2987">Formula</p>
<span class="katex-display"><span class="katex"><span class="katex-mathml"><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="block"><semantics><mrow><mi>A</mi><mi>c</mi><mi>c</mi><mi>e</mi><mi>s</mi><mi>s</mi><mi>T</mi><mi>i</mi><mi>m</mi><mi>e</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>S</mi><mi>e</mi><mi>e</mi><mi>k</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>R</mi><mi>o</mi><mi>t</mi><mi>a</mi><mi>t</mi><mi>i</mi><mi>o</mi><mi>n</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>T</mi><mi>r</mi><mi>a</mi><mi>n</mi><mi>s</mi><mi>f</mi><mi>e</mi><mi>r</mi></mrow><annotation encoding="application/x-tex">AccessTime
=
Seek
+
Rotation
+
Transfer</annotation></semantics></math></span><span class="katex-html" aria-hidden="true"><span class="base"><span class="strut"></span><span class="mord mathnormal">A</span><span class="mord mathnormal">ccess</span><span class="mord mathnormal">T</span><span class="mord mathnormal">im</span><span class="mord mathnormal">e</span><span class="mspace"></span><span class="mrel">=</span><span class="mspace"></span></span><span class="base"><span class="strut"></span><span class="mord mathnormal">S</span><span class="mord mathnormal">ee</span><span class="mord mathnormal">k</span><span class="mspace"></span><span class="mbin">+</span><span class="mspace"></span></span><span class="base"><span class="strut"></span><span class="mord mathnormal">R</span><span class="mord mathnormal">o</span><span class="mord mathnormal">t</span><span class="mord mathnormal">a</span><span class="mord mathnormal">t</span><span class="mord mathnormal">i</span><span class="mord mathnormal">o</span><span class="mord mathnormal">n</span><span class="mspace"></span><span class="mbin">+</span><span class="mspace"></span></span><span class="base"><span class="strut"></span><span class="mord mathnormal">T</span><span class="mord mathnormal">r</span><span class="mord mathnormal">an</span><span class="mord mathnormal">s</span><span class="mord mathnormal">f</span><span class="mord mathnormal">er</span></span></span></span></span>

<h1 data-section-id="mkv0x6" data-start="3466" data-end="3498">16. IEEE Research Paper Sample</h1>
<p data-start="3500" data-end="3612">This is probably the <strong data-start="3521" data-end="3549">best overall stress test</strong> because it naturally combines almost every formatting feature.</p>
<p data-start="3614" data-end="3626">It includes:</p>
<ul data-start="3628" data-end="3937">
<li data-section-id="16mauaw" data-start="3628" data-end="3638">
Abstract
</li>
<li data-section-id="ptaobm" data-start="3639" data-end="3649">
Keywords
</li>
<li data-section-id="1pq9wb9" data-start="3650" data-end="3675">
Multiple heading levels
</li>
<li data-section-id="qqhx0h" data-start="3676" data-end="3704">
<strong data-start="3678" data-end="3686">Bold</strong> and <em data-start="3691" data-end="3699">italic</em> text
</li>
<li data-section-id="1jzwefi" data-start="3705" data-end="3723">
Inline equations
</li>
<li data-section-id="1ops8v1" data-start="3724" data-end="3743">
Display equations
</li>
<li data-section-id="1dgkhzz" data-start="3744" data-end="3758">
Bullet lists
</li>
<li data-section-id="135cfff" data-start="3759" data-end="3775">
Numbered lists
</li>
<li data-section-id="5s09k5" data-start="3776" data-end="3784">
Tables
</li>
<li data-section-id="10l6ezy" data-start="3785" data-end="3808">
Figures with captions
</li>
<li data-section-id="c7xda7" data-start="3809" data-end="3822">
Code blocks
</li>
<li data-section-id="15vkgf0" data-start="3823" data-end="3856">
Citations like <strong data-start="3840" data-end="3847">[1]</strong>, <strong data-start="3849" data-end="3856">[2]</strong>
</li>
<li data-section-id="ripcs0" data-start="3857" data-end="3869">
References
</li>
<li data-section-id="1244rfd" data-start="3870" data-end="3881">
Footnotes
</li>
<li data-section-id="1n1bgaz" data-start="3882" data-end="3937">
Mixed paragraphs containing both text and mathematics
</li>

<hr data-start="3939" data-end="3942">
<h2 data-section-id="1tzun89" data-start="3944" data-end="3974">Ultimate Stress-Test Report</h2>
<p data-start="3976" data-end="4138">If your goal is to build something like FormatMagic, create a single <strong data-start="4045" data-end="4069">15 to 20 page report</strong> titled <strong data-start="4077" data-end="4115">"Modern Computer Science Handbook"</strong> with chapters such as:</p>
<ol data-start="4140" data-end="4437">
<li data-section-id="1vrv4fl" data-start="4140" data-end="4160">
Operating Systems
</li>
<li data-section-id="zy8j3n" data-start="4161" data-end="4181">
Computer Networks
</li>
<li data-section-id="1tk5o8b" data-start="4182" data-end="4201">
Database Systems
</li>
<li data-section-id="quwaau" data-start="4202" data-end="4221">
Software Testing
</li>
<li data-section-id="1vvm1an" data-start="4222" data-end="4240">
Compiler Design
</li>
<li data-section-id="yr0fji" data-start="4241" data-end="4267">
Artificial Intelligence
</li>
<li data-section-id="1wjdmp9" data-start="4268" data-end="4287">
Machine Learning
</li>
<li data-section-id="1bcxkt9" data-start="4288" data-end="4315">
Transformer Architecture
</li>
<li data-section-id="1amosqf" data-start="4316" data-end="4329">
Statistics
</li>
<li data-section-id="mgic2p" data-start="4330" data-end="4341">
Physics
</li>
<li data-section-id="1kwu8l8" data-start="4342" data-end="4368">
Electrical Engineering
</li>
<li data-section-id="7z29dm" data-start="4369" data-end="4381">
Calculus
</li>
<li data-section-id="1x4d9qa" data-start="4382" data-end="4405">
Digital Electronics
</li>
<li data-section-id="fg3i6w" data-start="4406" data-end="4422">
Cryptography
</li>
<li data-section-id="magjef" data-start="4423" data-end="4437">
References
</li>
</ol>
<p data-start="4439" data-end="4779" data-is-last-node="" data-is-only-node="">This one document will exercise virtually every parser in your pipeline: headings, nested lists, inline and display math, tables, code fences, citations, references, bold/italic text, and mixed-content paragraphs. If your formatter can preserve that cleanly from copy-paste, it's a far more convincing benchmark than a dozen simple reports.</p><!--EndFragment-->
</body>

<h1 data-section-id="mkv0x6" data-start="3466" data-end="3498">16. IEEE Research Paper Sample</h1>
<p data-start="3500" data-end="3612">This is probably the <strong data-start="3521" data-end="3549">best overall stress test</strong> because it naturally combines almost every formatting feature.</p>
<p data-start="3614" data-end="3626">It includes:</p>
<ul data-start="3628" data-end="3937">
<li data-section-id="16mauaw" data-start="3628" data-end="3638">
Abstract
</li>
<li data-section-id="ptaobm" data-start="3639" data-end="3649">
Keywords
</li>
<li data-section-id="1pq9wb9" data-start="3650" data-end="3675">
Multiple heading levels
</li>
<li data-section-id="qqhx0h" data-start="3676" data-end="3704">
<strong data-start="3678" data-end="3686">Bold</strong> and <em data-start="3691" data-end="3699">italic</em> text
</li>
<li data-section-id="1jzwefi" data-start="3705" data-end="3723">
Inline equations
</li>
<li data-section-id="1ops8v1" data-start="3724" data-end="3743">
Display equations
</li>
<li data-section-id="1dgkhzz" data-start="3744" data-end="3758">
Bullet lists
</li>
<li data-section-id="135cfff" data-start="3759" data-end="3775">
Numbered lists
</li>
<li data-section-id="5s09k5" data-start="3776" data-end="3784">
Tables
</li>
<li data-section-id="10l6ezy" data-start="3785" data-end="3808">
Figures with captions
</li>
<li data-section-id="c7xda7" data-start="3809" data-end="3822">
Code blocks
</li>
<li data-section-id="15vkgf0" data-start="3823" data-end="3856">
Citations like <strong data-start="3840" data-end="3847">[1]</strong>, <strong data-start="3849" data-end="3856">[2]</strong>
</li>
<li data-section-id="ripcs0" data-start="3857" data-end="3869">
References
</li>
<li data-section-id="1244rfd" data-start="3870" data-end="3881">
Footnotes
</li>
<li data-section-id="1n1bgaz" data-start="3882" data-end="3937">
Mixed paragraphs containing both text and mathematics
</li>
</ul>

<h2 data-section-id="1tzun89" data-start="3944" data-end="3974">Ultimate Stress-Test Report</h2>
<p data-start="3976" data-end="4138">If your goal is to build something like FormatMagic, create a single <strong data-start="4045" data-end="4069">15 to 20 page report</strong> titled <strong data-start="4077" data-end="4115">"Modern Computer Science Handbook"</strong> with chapters such as:</p>
<ol data-start="4140" data-end="4437">
<li data-section-id="1vrv4fl" data-start="4140" data-end="4160">
Operating Systems
</li>
<li data-section-id="zy8j3n" data-start="4161" data-end="4181">
Computer Networks
</li>
<li data-section-id="1tk5o8b" data-start="4182" data-end="4201">
Database Systems
</li>
<li data-section-id="quwaau" data-start="4202" data-end="4221">
Software Testing
</li>
<li data-section-id="1vvm1an" data-start="4222" data-end="4240">
Compiler Design
</li>
<li data-section-id="yr0fji" data-start="4241" data-end="4267">
Artificial Intelligence
</li>
<li data-section-id="1wjdmp9" data-start="4268" data-end="4287">
Machine Learning
</li>
<li data-section-id="1bcxkt9" data-start="4288" data-end="4315">
Transformer Architecture
</li>
<li data-section-id="1amosqf" data-start="4316" data-end="4329">
Statistics
</li>
<li data-section-id="mgic2p" data-start="4330" data-end="4341">
Physics
</li>
<li data-section-id="1kwu8l8" data-start="4342" data-end="4368">
Electrical Engineering
</li>
<li data-section-id="7z29dm" data-start="4369" data-end="4381">
Calculus
</li>
<li data-section-id="1x4d9qa" data-start="4382" data-end="4405">
Digital Electronics
</li>
<li data-section-id="fg3i6w" data-start="4406" data-end="4422">
Cryptography
</li>
<li data-section-id="magjef" data-start="4423" data-end="4437">
References
</li>
</ol>
<p data-start="4439" data-end="4779" data-is-last-node="" data-is-only-node="">This one document will exercise virtually every parser in your pipeline: headings, nested lists, inline and display math, tables, code fences, citations, references, bold/italic text, and mixed-content paragraphs. If your formatter can preserve that cleanly from copy-paste, it's a far more convincing benchmark than a dozen simple reports.</p><!--EndFragment-->
